t_ch: 一听就是有故事的人。
t_en: Someone who clearly has stories to tell.

t_ch: 「写下黑塔序列的最后一行时，我曾短暂看到过另一个世界。它是如此简洁，就连觐见博识尊的感觉也比不上那种精妙。但…」天才凝望身下来来往往的学士，目光停留在更远的地方——
t_en: "When I wrote down the last line of Herta's sequence, I momentarily saw another world. It was so utterly simple, and even the feeling of meeting Nous pales in comparison to its intricacy. But..." The genius gazes down at the bustling scholars below, her eyes lingering at a place even farther away...

t_ch: 一座坍塌的实验室，一座停留在她记忆中的，象征失败与耻辱的废墟。
t_en: A collapsed lab. A pile of debris symbolizing failure and shame that remains eternal in her memories.

t_ch: 又一次意外？
t_en: Another accident?

t_ch: 「爆炸来自于实验室里的粒子。它在两种状态间高速转换，迅速释放出能量——简单来说，这颗再普通不过的粒子，在毫无征兆的情况下变成了炸弹。」
t_en: "The explosion was caused by the lab's particle. It changed between two states at high speeds, rapidly generating energy — simply put, without any signs, this absolutely ordinary particle became a bomb in a circumstance where it couldn't have happened."

t_ch: 「当我从废墟里醒来，灵光一闪时看到的景象，已消失得无影无踪。」你少有从黑塔的目光中看到愤怒，「我找到一根离开知识圆圈的绳子，然后，它被切断了。」
t_en: "When I woke from the debris, the scenario that I saw when inspiration struck me had vanished completely from my sight." You rarely detect such anger in Herta's eyes. "I found a rope that could lead me beyond the Circle of Knowledge, and then it got cut off."

t_ch: 「但它关不住我的，天才不可能永远困在圆圈里。」
t_en: "But it can't shut me in. Geniuses won't allow themselves to be shackled in the circle."

t_ch: 学士们的歌声愈发高昂，它拂过伤员的面庞，飘出伊斯梅尔的过滤层，一直飞到许多个琥珀纪后的时光。
t_en: The scholars' song becomes more sonorous. It grazes past the faces of the wounded, drifting beyond Izmael's filtration layer, till it flies into a time many Amber Eras later.

t_ch: 侧耳倾听。
t_en: Listen closely.

t_ch: 「就让自命清高的大师为自己写颂歌吧！让他们以学派的名义画地为牢。我们是无能的庸人，但庸人的智慧却比智者更宽广！」
t_en: "Let the self-righteous masters write their own hymns! Let them imprison themselves in the name of their schools. We are incompetent Mundanites, but a Mundanite's intellect is broader than an erudite's!"

t_ch: 音乐家赛梅在抗议期间谱写的庸人曲，为齐唱的学士们提供了灵感。
t_en: The musician Saime writes a Mundanite melody during the resistance, providing inspiration for the scholars singing in chorus.

t_ch: 等到抗议结束，成功的果实酿成美酒。等到智慧不再受约束，知识也失去价码。到那时，真理将遁入尘烟，到那时，<color=#dbc291ff>庸众院</color>的大门将向每位求知者敞开。
t_en: When the resistance ends, the fruits of success will become fine wine. When intellect is no longer restricted, knowledge will lose its value. When that happens, truth will be buried in dust and smoke. When that happens, the <color=#dbc291ff>Council of Mundanites</color> will open its gates to any and all who seek knowledge.

t_ch: 边星克罗斯特亚，云翳上的投影，<color=#dbc291ff>相对认知学派</color>的伊格莱情绪激昂，演讲在共计<unbreak>142</unbreak>个星域同步播出。
t_en: On the border star Klostia, projected across the nebulae, Eagleye of the <color=#dbc291ff>School of Relative Cognition</color> orates fervently, their monologue simultaneously broadcast across <unbreak>142</unbreak> segmenta.

t_ch: 「『知识』是编造的谎言，战争是争夺话语权的游戏。」博识学会在宇宙中建立了一种暴政，那就是，<color=#dbc291ff>只有他们能决定什么是正确！</color>
t_en: "Knowledge is a fabricated lie, and war is a game to seize the right to have your voice heard." The Intelligentsia Guild has established a despotic rule over the universe, that is — <color=#dbc291ff>only they get to decide what is right!</color>

t_ch: 你是一个落魄的学士…难民，或二者皆是的某个流浪汉。你蹒跚走进帝皇战争的重建区，只想求得三两口粮，养活家里的弟弟妹妹。伊格莱的信徒们焚烧图书，分发印有他画像的传单，他们将其戏称为：学派战争的入场券。
t_en: You are a disheveled scholar... a refugee, or a vagrant who's kind of both. You hobble into the reconstructed zone of the Emperor's War, seeking a mere few morsels of sustenance, to feed your siblings at home. Eagleye's zealots are razing books and handing out fliers in his image. They derisively refer to these papers as: admission tickets for the Scholars' Strife.

t_ch: 收下传单。
t_en: Take the flier.

t_ch: 「联觉信标控制我们听到的一切，博识学会全都是一群骗子！」他们不再相信任何人。
t_en: "The Synesthesia Beacon controls all that we hear. The Intelligentsia Guild members are all liars!" They have lost trust in everyone.

t_ch: 拒绝传单。
t_en: Decline the flier.

t_ch: 「能源危机是公司塑造的假象，否则，为什么那么多人衣食无忧，那么多人却饿死街头？」他们愤慨地吼道。
t_en: "The Energy Crisis is just a façade fabricated by IPC. If it were real, why would there be so many people living without concern while others starve in the streets?" They roar angrily.

t_ch: 只要有利可图，他们可以把红定义成蓝，白定义成黑。如若如此，那每个人都应有定义知识的权力！
t_en: As long as it benefits them, they can say that red is blue and black is white! If that's the case, then everyone should have the right to define what knowledge is!

t_ch: 「我见寰宇本没有完美学者，亦或满大街都是完美学者！」在伊格莱振聋发聩的吼声里，吞噬弹炸毁了学会驻地的外墙。你们一拥而上，要让他们也体会到应得的苦难。现在，每个人都可以是相对认知学派的学士。
t_en: "If you ask me, either there are no perfect scholars in the cosmos, or everyone is a perfect scholar!" Through the sound of Eagleye's deafening roars, a devouring bomb obliterates the Guild hold's outer wall. You all charge forward as one, ready to make them feel the suffering they deserve. Now, everyone can become a scholar of the School of Relative Cognition.

t_ch: 为宇宙带来真实。
t_en: Let's bring truth to this universe!

t_ch: 「星神根本就不存在，一切都是假的！」你怒吼。
t_en: "Aeons don't exist! It is all a lie!!" You roar in a ferocious rage.

t_ch: 把骗子通通赶走。
t_en: Chase out every last deceiver!

t_ch: 「学士们才是能源战争的根源，我们全都被蒙在鼓里！」你呐喊。
t_en: "Scholars are the true origin of the energy war, and they've kept us all in the dark!" You shout.

t_ch: 支援的炮舰令天空失色，清算的机械落下，在各大学派的相互攻讦之前，必须掐灭相对认知的火星。你们试图撤出克罗斯特亚，但围剿的军团已将这里堵得水泄不通。
t_en: As the supporting fire of the cannon ships darken the sky, the liquidation machines fall to the ground. Before the various schools can turn to expose each other's past misdeeds, the spark of Relative Cognition must be extinguished. You attempt to withdraw from Klostia, but the encircling Legion have blocked all escape.

t_ch: 在基地沦陷的前一秒，你按下了「权杖」的指令输入按钮，作为无声的呐喊。
t_en: One second before the base is conquered, you press the Scepter control button as your silent battle cry.

t_ch: 筏工书法欧奇是否被。
t_en: Far gong shoo far oh chee shoo fow bay?

t_ch: 你无法再理解耳畔的话语。
t_en: Suddenly, you can no longer understand a single word being spoken around you.

t_ch: 「权杖」摧毁了整个星域的联觉信标，你回到了没有欺骗与谎言的时代。在星间蔓延的呓语中，烛墨学派捕捉到谜语的气息，可他们也不清楚，记录本身是否也染上「神秘」。
t_en: The Scepter destroyed the Synesthesia Beacons active in the entire quadrant. You have once again returned to a time without deceit. Inside the gibberish that spreads among the stars, the Candelagraphos caught the scent of riddles, but they could not tell if even the records themselves had been tainted by Enigmata.

t_ch: 你是星河涟漪号的导航智能，你指引星船穿越失去语言的克罗斯特亚，向着旧帝国的疆域深处航行。帝皇虽死，藏匿的反有机方程孑遗，却仍可能暗中将你纳入他们的运算进程。
t_en: You are the navigation intelligence for the galaxy-roaming ship, Rippling Waves. You guide the ship through the language-less Klostia, deep into the territory of the old empire. Though the Emperor is dead, there may be remnants of the Anti-Organic Equation that may secretly factor you into their calculations.

t_ch: 决策智能黑塔女士提议，最好把飞船换成自动驾驶。好吧，你就是那个自动驾驶程序。
t_en: The strategic intelligence, Madam Herta, proposed that it may be best to have the ship run on autopilot. So, you are the resulting autopilot program.

t_ch: 自动驾驶很酷好吧！
t_en: Autopilot is cool, okay?

t_ch: 凭什么黑塔是决策智能？
t_en: Why should Herta be the strategic intelligence?

t_ch: 我也要做决策智能！
t_en: I want to be the strategic intelligence!

t_ch: 这趟危险的旅途仅有一名旅客，名为帕提维娅的学士。只有学会的疯子，才敢于孤身飞往铸铁王座的方向。
t_en: There is only one passenger aboard for this perilous journey, a scholar by the name of Patavia. Only lunatics from the Guild dare to venture towards the Iron Throne alone.

t_ch: 她给了你们一个模型，坐标在星图上随时间高速移动，一时难以锚定。
t_en: She provided you with a model. The coordinates of the target move rapidly around the astral chart as time passes, making it hard to anchor.

t_ch: 「这道孤波，或许它只是个无意义的现象。」帕提维娅对即将面对的答案心感不安，她看似自言自语，却又希望冰冷的机器能做出回应。
t_en: "Perhaps this solitary wave is just a meaningless phenomenon..." Patavia seemed nervous about the answer that awaited her. She seemed to be half talking to herself, half hoping the cold machinery before her could give her a response.

t_ch: 请问您需要帮助吗？
t_en: Can I help you at all?

t_ch: 是否需要检索相关服务？
t_en: Would you like me to search for related services?

t_ch: 「不…就当我在说胡话吧。我很害怕，总感觉，自己一直在被某种力量推着往前…也可能是我自作多情。」在对模型进行了多次迭代后，帕提维娅成功实现了对涟漪发生源的反向追踪。
t_en: "No... Just ignore me. I'm just so scared. I feel that some force has been driving me forward... Although maybe I'm just overthinking it." After recalibrating the model several times, Patavia successfully manages to reverse-navigate the source of the waves.

t_ch: 「知识圆圈，你们或许听过这个概念。但，假如，我是说假如，我们自以为走出了圆圈，实际却一步也没能离开…」相对认知学派的话语渗进帕提维娅的思维，自我怀疑的种子开枝散叶——怎样才能证明自己的正确？在成果出现以前。
t_en: "The Circle of Knowledge... Perhaps you've heard of this concept before? Although, hypothetically... I'm only talking hypothetically, what if we just think that we have stepped outside our Circle of Knowledge, but actually have not managed to escape at all...?" The words of the School of Relative Cognition seep into Patavia's thoughts as seeds of self-doubt begin to sprout... How can one prove that they are correct before results are seen?

t_ch: 你和目的地的移动轨迹逐渐同步，模型中涟漪的整体结构也愈发清晰。系统的另一侧，化身决策智能的黑塔「面色」凝重。
t_en: As your trajectory begins to sync with your destination's movement, the Rippling Waves' overall structure in the model grows clearer. On the other end of the system, Herta, who had become the strategic intelligence, has her "face" grow solemn.

t_ch: 「烛墨学派居然没把这么重要的东西记下来，他们是瞎子吗？！」星船因她的情绪变化上下颠簸。
t_en: "How could the Candelagraphos not record something as important as this? Are they blind!?" The ship began to shake under the turbulence of her emotions.

t_ch: 「那道涟漪的起点，是<color=#dbc291ff>博识尊演算的『时刻』！</color>那是…星神思考时的驻波。」
t_en: "The origin of these waves is an <color=#dbc291ff>"Instant" extrapolated by Nous!</color> That's the standing wave generated from an Aeon's thought!"

t_ch: 一颗隐形的星星。二世曾认为自己把它藏得很好，被虚数能量包裹，却又恰到好处地处于内外平衡状态，即使花上千纪的时间，围绕王座的卫星也不会被人发现。
t_en: A hidden star. Rubert II believed he had it perfectly concealed, enveloped in Imaginary energy but also a perfect state of internal and external balance. One could search for thousands of Amber Eras and never find this satellite that circled the Throne.

t_ch: 但它恰到好处地形成一个「巧合」，混沌中的不动点，与祂的「时刻」完美重合。因此，继鲁珀特二世之后，第一位有机生命踏上了它的土地。
t_en: But it just so happened to form a "coincidence." This stable point in the chaos perfectly overlaps with THEIR "Instant." That is how the first organic life form stepped upon this land after Rubert II passed.

t_ch: 帕提维娅走出飞船，不敢相信眼前的景象。金属密布的废墟，鲁珀特一世在机械坟场中醒来，愤怒萦绕着他的回路。那将二世又如何？
t_en: Patavia walks out of the ship, in complete disbelief at the scene before her eyes. Among the metal-covered ruins, Rubert I awoke from the mechanical graveyard, rage lingering in his circuits. What then would become of Rubert II?

t_ch: 她看到一片花海，盛开在无机物上的，有机生命的花朵。高大的倒三角墓碑屹立于中央，轮廓残破。建造者独自将坟场中的金属堆砌，一点点地打造成眼前的墓碑。
t_en: She sees a vast field of flowers. Organic floral life is blooming atop the inorganic. In the center, a gigantic upside-down triangular tombstone looms, casting a silhouette of disrepair. The constructor piled up all the metal in the graveyard alone, creating the tombstones you see before you bit by bit.

t_ch: 为坟场的智械而建。
t_en: Built for the Intellitrons in the graveyard.

t_ch: 为寰宇的生灵而建。
t_en: Built for life across the universe.

t_ch: 为已死的帝皇而建。
t_en: Built for the deceased Emperor.

t_ch: 就仿佛，鲁珀特二世早已预料到自己的死亡。特地在王座边缘，为自己留下这座不被打扰的<color=#dbc291ff>衣冠冢</color>。
t_en: It was as if Rubert II had long-since predicted their own death. As if they had purposely left this <color=#dbc291ff>cenotaph</color> around the edge of the throne so as not to be disturbed.

t_ch: 帕提维娅小心翼翼地走上前。在墓碑前，她看到一束别致的无机花，花蕾是亚共合金，枝干是晶体管和铜丝，铁锈和机油的芬芳与花香混合在一起。
t_en: Patavia cautiously steps forward. In front of the tombstone, she sees a unique bouquet of inorganic flowers. Their buds are constructed from acrylic alloys, their stems of transistors and copper wire, with the smell of rust and machine oil mixed with their floral scent.

t_ch: 她调用出溯源模型，但那神秘的信号已悄然消失，只因她已身处风暴的中心。她花了很长的时间来消化这个事实，恒星从头顶跌落了三次。然后，她不得不花费更长的时间去消化另一个事实。
t_en: She activates the tracing model, but the mysterious signal has disappeared, as she is already in the eye of the storm. It takes so long for her to digest the fact that the star above her head falls under the horizon three times. Then, she spends even longer trying to digest yet another fact.

t_ch: 在孤波的源头，<color=#dbc291ff>「权杖」系统的核心</color>，便是这颗星星本身。
t_en: The star itself, at the origin of the solitary wave, is the <color=#dbc291ff>Scepter system core.</color>

t_ch: 学派「战争」时常被史学家们批评名不副实。与动辄数十纪的诸多事件相比，它的时间太过短暂，局限于博识学会内部的冲突，亦缺乏对银河的深远影响，就像一只羸弱的蝴蝶。
t_en: The influence of the scholastic "Strife" is often deemed fake by historians. When compared with events that spanned multiple Eras, it was too brief and limited only to the internal clashes of the Intelligentsia Guild. It lacked substantial influence on the Cosmos, like the pathetic wings of a weak butterfly.

t_ch: 站在「权杖」的核心面前。惊恐、亢奋，肾上腺素和多巴胺刺激情绪，令思想短暂地定格。帕提维娅没来由地感到愤怒，被无知遮住双眼的愤怒，她失去所有的欲望，只剩下求知的冲动。
t_en: Standing before the core of the Scepter, fear, excitement, adrenaline, and dopamine send her emotions racing and stop her from thinking clearly. Patavia becomes clouded in an irrational rage, a rage that blinds her with ignorance. She loses all desire, and all that remains is an urge for knowledge.

t_ch: 所谓的无机仿生神经元集群，不过是对博识尊的拙劣模仿。帝皇制造出天体级的计算干涉装置，给予其解答的算力，却没有赋予它们「提问」的机能。
t_en: These so-called inorganic neuron clusters are merely poor imitations of Nous. The Emperor constructed celestial-level computational interference devices and gave them the computational power to answer questions, yet never gave them the function to "ask."

t_ch: 工具无法替帝皇思考。
t_en: Tools could never replace the Emperor's thought.

t_ch: 工具不配替帝皇思考。
t_en: Tools are not worthy to replace the Emperor's thought.

t_ch: 不求问于任何个体，只依靠自己的大脑。正因如此，他才是帝皇，无需星神权能的授勋。无限延续的方程，遍布寰宇的视线，以及受「权杖」扩展的思维，他将自己塑造成理想的完美学者。
t_en: To never question any other being, but simply rely on one's own mind. That is precisely why he was the Emperor, why he never required the honor of Aeonic authority. With an infinitely expanding equation that spreads across the galaxy and the thought expansion provided by the Scepters, he made himself into his ideal of the perfect scholar.

t_ch: 他是「权杖」组成的神经系统中，唯一的细胞核。他在铸铁的王座上，<color=#dbc291ff>自我加冕！</color>
t_en: He was the only cell nucleus in the nervous system formed by the Scepters. He <color=#dbc291ff>crowned himself</color> upon the Iron Throne!

t_ch: 鲁珀特二世死了，自我加冕走向了终结。如果他思考继续下去会怎样？走出知识圆圈，创造出一个<color=#dbc291ff>知识奇点</color>，改写宇宙知识定义的反生命方程……
t_en: Rubert II died, and his self-appointed reign came to an end. What if his thought had continued? If he had left the Circle of Knowledge and created a <color=#dbc291ff>Knowledge Singularity</color>, rewriting the antilife equation that alters the definition of universal knowledge...

t_ch: 他会杀死所有的生命。
t_en: He would have killed all life.

t_ch: 有机和无机，所有的生命都会消失。
t_en: All life, organic and inorganic, would have disappeared.

t_ch: 他将创造全新的生命。
t_en: He would have created a new form of life.

t_ch: 伟大的自由意志，将超越生命的束缚。
t_en: Almighty free will would surpass the shackles of life.

t_ch: 帕提维娅不由被这般猜想震慑。如果「权杖」系统是帝皇扩展思维的工具，同样，自己也能向它们提问，扩展自己的大脑——加冕为预言中的天才。
t_en: Patavia is shaken by this hypothesis. If the Scepter system is the Emperor's tool to expand their mind, then surely she could also question them to expand her mind in the same way... To be crowned as the genius of prophecy.

t_ch: 帕提维娅将意识接入核心。超距作用下，帝国疆域内所有的「权杖」瞬间连接成完整的神经系统，侍奉它们久违的新王。
t_en: Patavia connects her consciousness to the core. In an instant, all Scepters across the vast distance of the Empire reform into a complete nervous system, ready to serve their long-awaited new ruler.

t_ch: 可就在发问前的瞬间，一缕自我怀疑却陡然摄住她的心神。她抬头看向花海深处，一缕香甜的气息和一抹糖果色的衣影飘过。
t_en: However, just as she is about to ask her question, a sliver of self-doubt seizes her mind. She lifts her head to look out into the field of flowers, as a sweet scent and a glimpse of pastel-colored robes drift by.

t_ch: 她曾与那人交谈过数次，那位缥缈的女士，面目模糊，却言简意赅。她的语词如手术刀般锋锐精准，总是切开困惑，解去迷障，也叫她心生自惭形秽。
t_en: Patavia had spoken with that person a number of times before. That floating and insubstantial woman with an indiscernible face, but concise and absolute speech. Her words are piercing and precise like a surgeon's scalpel, always cutting through Patavia's confusion and resolving Patavia's self-doubt, whilst filling the scholar with a sense of inferiority.

t_ch: 「凭我的智慧…真能『自我加冕』吗？」毕竟，她是庸人，而非天才。
t_en: "Can I really 'crown myself' with my mere level of intelligence...?" She is only a Mundanite after all. She is no genius.

t_ch: 以外部时间看，帕提维娅迟疑了数秒左右。然后，她放弃了以「权杖」自我加冕，选择将目光向上迁移，看向<color=#dbc291ff>「智识」的尽头</color>。
t_en: Patavia only hesitates for a few seconds in terms of external time. Then, she gives up on crowning herself with the Scepters and decides to move her gaze upwards, fixing on the <color=#dbc291ff>end of the Erudition</color> above.

t_ch: 我想让后人捕捉祂的天慧…
t_en: I want future generations to capture THEIR divine wisdom...

t_ch: 我想领受祂的片刻思考…
t_en: I wish to receive a brief moment of THEIR thoughts...

t_ch: 那就…如她所愿。「权杖」高扬，轻点凡人颅顶，赞颂求知之人，她必偿所愿。
t_en: Then... As she wishes. The Scepters rise high and gently tap themselves on the mortal's head. Those who seek and glorify knowledge shall receive what they desire.

t_ch: 模拟宇宙的机能有限，无法演算帕提维娅看到的场景。仅是从她的认知中捕捉到的离散数据，就险些让模拟宇宙崩溃重启。
t_en: The programming functions of the Simulated Universe are limited and are incapable of extrapolating the scene that Patavia sees before her. Just the scattered data captured from her cognition nearly causes the Simulated Universe to stall and reboot.

t_ch: 在「智识」的思维世界，她看到了梦寐以求的那片海洋。从最初捕捉到的信号，到完整的孤波，虚数背景中的涟漪，它们在海洋里，也仅是涟漪下的涟漪。
t_en: She sees the great ocean that she had dreamed of in the vast thoughts of the Erudition. From the first signals she captured to the entire solitary wave to the Rippling Waves in the Imaginary background — When placed inside this great ocean, are no more than waves below the waves.

t_ch: 你无法要求刀耕火种的人学习反物质引擎的原理。哪怕为他们展示了聚变炉的火花，也没人会把它和头顶的恒星相互联系。
t_en: You cannot ask a simple farmer to understand the principles of the antimatter engine. Even if you were to show them the sparks of the fusion furnace, no one could relate it to the stars that burn above their heads.

t_ch: 简而言之——<color=#dbc291ff>帕提维娅无法理解眼前的知识</color>。她无法理解想要解答的问题！她看不懂！
t_en: Put simply —<color=#dbc291ff>Patavia cannot comprehend the knowledge before her</color>. She cannot understand the question she wants to answer! She cannot understand!

t_ch: 无法走出圆圈的失败者。
t_en: A failure who could not escape the Circle of Knowledge.

t_ch: 无法成为天才的凡庸者。
t_en: A Mundanite who could never become a genius.

t_ch: 无法触及真理的无知者。
t_en: A fool who could never encounter the truth.

t_ch: 她希冀将眼前海洋般广阔的知识放入大脑，但她的智慧仅是海中的一颗水分子，难以容纳如此庞大的信息。
t_en: She wishes to absorb the vast ocean of knowledge before her into her mind, but her intelligence is only the scope of a single water particle in the ocean. There is no way it can contain such a vast wealth of information.

t_ch: 博识尊向思维的窥探者投下视线，祂沉默不语，一如往常。遍布帝皇疆域的「权杖」逐级瘫痪，知识自诸多疑问中流出，流向它们唯一的终点。
t_en: Nous looks down upon the one who pried upon THEIR thoughts, remaining silent as ever. The Scepters throughout the Emperor's domain become paralyzed level by level as the knowledge of countless questions leaks out, flowing towards their singular destination.

t_ch: 帕提维娅麻木地记录下眼前的知识，将它们蛮横地塞入脑中。她太过无力，甚至无法分清，知识究竟在被自己主动记下，还是由博识尊灌输进她的意识。
t_en: Patavia robotically records down all the knowledge before her and stuffs it brutally into her mind. She has so little agency in the matter that she cannot even tell whether it is her actively recording the knowledge, or Nous is forcibly instilling knowledge into her consciousness.

t_ch: <unbreak>0.1</unbreak>秒后，最后的核心成为了废铁。
t_en: <unbreak>0.1</unbreak> seconds later, the final core becomes scrap metal.

t_ch: 帕提维娅趴在地上，发疯似的寻找能刻下公式的地方，她攥住生锈的铁片，刨开花簇，拼命在金属上留下脑中转瞬即逝的奥秘，那道涟漪。她无法通晓公式的含义，只是凭借仅剩的记忆，在精疲力竭前，将它们写满坟场的每个角落。
t_en: Patavia lies face down on the ground, maniacally searching for a place to carve down the formulae she had seen. She grabs a rusty iron sheet, peels open the bouquet of flowers, noting down the truths that are escaping out of her mind by the second, that wave of ripple, as if her life depends on it. She cannot grasp the meaning of the formula. She is merely relying on her lingering memories to scrawl them across every inch of the graveyard before succumbing to exhaustion.

t_ch: 很多年后，帕提维娅学士成为了帕提维娅博士，她留下的公式成为了名垂千古的<color=#dbc291ff>孤波算法难题</color>。它困扰了寰宇近百个琥珀纪的时间，直到一位名为<color=#dbc291ff>黑塔</color>的少女将它解开，跻身天才的行列。
t_en: Many years later, Patavia the scholar becomes Doctor Patavia. The formula she leaves behind becomes the famous <color=#dbc291ff>solitary waves theory</color>. This conundrum confounded the Cosmos for almost a hundred Amber Eras until the day that a young lady named <color=#dbc291ff>Herta</color> solved it, elevating herself to the rank of geniuses.

t_ch: 「孤波算法难题，它让我成为了天才，奠定了模拟宇宙的演算基础。从这个角度看，声势浩大的学派战争，鲁珀特二世的遗产，实际上都不过是孤波算法难题的注脚。」
t_en: "The solitary waves theory allowed me to become a genius and laid the foundations for the extrapolation of the Simulated Universe. Looking at it this way, the dramatic airs of the Scholars' Strife and Rubert II's legacy are no more than footnotes for the solitary waves theory."

t_ch: 「帕提维娅博士刻苦钻研，与好友通力合作，最终提出了孤波算法难题。人们盛赞提出问题的她，也惋惜不能解开难题的她……至少我读过的书是这样写的。」
t_en: "Doctor Patavia dedicated herself to grueling research, collaborated with friends and finally proposed the solitary waves theory. People both praised her for being the one to have posed the question and pitied her for being unable to solve it... At least, that's what it said in the books I read."

t_ch: 数千台「权杖」被学士们的问题占满，耗尽算力，成为太空垃圾。「权杖」系统全线瘫痪之后，学派间放下隔阂，重现回到了交流合作的和谐氛围，学者笔下的历史如此言说。因荒唐的预言而起，留一地鸡毛而散。
t_en: The thousands of Scepters were completely overrun with the questions of scholars, exhausting their computational power and turning them into space trash. Once the Scepter system was completely crippled, the harmonious atmosphere of communication and cooperation returned between Schools, according to the history recorded by scholars. It began with an absurd prophecy and ended with meaningless waste.

t_ch: 「…我现在才明白，这才是学会想要虚构史学家藏起来的东西。」
t_en: "...It is only now that I realize... this is what the Guild wants History Fictionologists to conceal."

t_ch: 庸人和天才间的天堑…
t_en: The natural chasm between the mundane and genius...

t_ch: 即使借用鲁珀特二世的头脑，即使窥视博识尊的思维，庸人仍旧无法成为天才。对于一生求知的学者来说，何其绝望……
t_en: Even after borrowing Rubert II's brain and peeking into Nous' vast knowledge, a Mundanite still cannot become a genius. How despairing it would be for a scholar who has spent their life seeking knowledge...

t_ch: 「而这『不可知域』……」
t_en: "And this 'Unknowable Domain'..."

t_ch: 「感谢你…帕提维娅博士。你曾跨越时间留下问题，而我给出了解答。但当我面对问题时，你再度跨越时间给了我解答的灵感——即便我们从未谋面。」
t_en: "Thank you... Doctor Patavia. You crossed the boundaries of time to leave a question, then I solved it. But when I faced my own unsolvable question, you once again transcended time to inspire me to answer it... Though we never even met."

t_ch: 「我们都被关在了知识圆圈里，看到的都只是现象，而非本质。」
t_en: "We are all trapped inside the Circle of Knowledge. All we see are phenomena, not reality."

t_ch: 「从更高的世界看清信号，它的本质是一道涟漪。如果被『不可知域』的迷雾遮蔽视线，那么我们要做的，就是走出障目的知识圆圈……」
t_en: "Looking from a higher world, you can see the signal's true essence is a rippling wave. If your vision is blocked by the mist of the Unknowable Domain, then one must move out of the obstruction of the Circle of Knowledge..."

t_ch: 「去更高的世界俯瞰！」
t_en: "Go to a higher world and look down upon it!"

t_ch: 碧蓝的光晕停留在地平线上，垂垂老矣的妇人伏在窗前，门扉敞开。一名陌生的少女穿过学会的层层封锁，径直来到了帕提维娅的病房。
t_en: As a sapphire halo hangs over the horizon, a frail old woman leans through her window, her door wide open. An unfamiliar girl passes through layers and layers of Guild blockades, making her way directly to Patavia's hospital ward.

t_ch: 她多半也是来问那些无聊问题的，老人心想。孤波算法难题，要么，就是追问博识尊和帝皇的线索。
t_en: She's probably here to ask me the same old boring questions, the old lady thinks to herself. Something about the solitary waves theory or clues on how to locate Nous and the Emperor.

t_ch: 「即将向『权杖』提问的时候，你放弃了。」她为什么会知道这个？是来带走自己的死神吗？可惜，无情的宇宙不会允许灵魂的存在。
t_en: "You gave up just as you were about to pose your question to the 'Scepters.'" How did she know that? Is she death, come to take me away? Unfortunately, the ruthless universe will not allow for the existence of a soul.

t_ch: 她无数次诘问自己，如果当时走上自我加冕的道路，结局是否会有所不同？庸人与天才的距离，真的无法跨越吗？一定是自己太过平庸。如果接入核心的是那些更伟大的学士……或许他们就能解开那道难题。
t_en: She had asked herself this question over and over already: Would things have ended differently if she had chosen to crown herself? Is the distance between mundanity and genius really insurmountable? It must have just been that she was too unremarkable. Perhaps if one of the greater scholars of the time had connected to the nucleus... Maybe they could have solved the conundrum themselves.

t_ch: 一定是这样。
t_en: Definitely.

t_ch: 不…也许他们也是徒劳。
t_en: No... Perhaps their efforts would have been in vain, also.

t_ch: 「我不是来提问的。」少女坐在妇人的床边，将一叠写满公式的稿纸放到她的身前，「我只是希望你能看看这个。」
t_en: "I did not come to ask you anything." The girl sits by the old lady's bedside and places a manuscript filled with formulas in front of her. "I just hoped that you would take a look at this."

t_ch: 这是闹哪出？老人有些困惑，可当她看到公式的第二行时，窥视星神时那莫大的震撼又重新笼罩住她的心灵——那是已证明完成的孤波算法难题。
t_en: What is going on here? The old woman is perplexed. But when she sees the second line of the formula, the overwhelming shock that enveloped her soul when she glimpsed the Aeon once again washed over her... The solitary waves theory has been solved.

t_ch: 「你是学士吗？还是…被祂所注视的天才？」请务必要是学士，生平第一次，帕提维娅向看不见的存在反复祈祷。
t_en: "Are you a scholar? Or... Have THEY looked upon you as a genius?" Please be a scholar... For the first time in her life, Patavia prays over and over to some unseen entity.

t_ch: 「……」少女沉默片刻，「学士。和您一样，提出问题，解答问题的学士。」
t_en: "..." The girl remains silent for a moment. "A scholar. Like you, I am a scholar that poses and answers questions."

t_ch: 「这样啊…」帕提维娅闭上眼睛，她想要哭出来，想要呐喊，可当语言从舌尖涌出，却化作一声叹息。
t_en: "I see..." Patavia closes her eyes, wanting to cry and scream, but when the words leave her lips, they form only a gentle sigh.

t_ch: 它太美了…
t_en: It's beautiful...

t_ch: 谢谢你骗了我。
t_en: Thank you for lying to me.

t_ch: 病榻上的老人有种预感，她从未与眼前的少女相会，此后她们也将不再相逢。在那之前，她想要问问面前这位向她展示造物之美的天才，「且不论那个变化的常数…年轻人，在你看来，宇宙的未来会是确定的，还是混沌的？」
t_en: The frail old lady on her deathbed has a vague premonition that, not only had she never met the girl in front of her before, but she will never meet with her again. Before this meeting ended, she wants to ask this genius that showed her the beauty of creation... "Putting aside the changing constant... Youngster, in your opinion, is the future of the universe predetermined, or chaotic?"

t_ch: 「谁知道呢？」少女的回答出乎意料。
t_en: "Who knows?" The young woman's reply is unexpected.

t_ch: 「说不定，就连博识尊自己，也想知道这个问题的答案。」
t_en: "Perhaps even Nous THEMSELF would like to know the answer to that question."

t_ch: 「这样啊……」垂垂老矣的妇人道，语声越来越低。
t_en: "I see..." Responds the frail old lady, her voice growing quieter.

t_ch: 星空之上，有一颗星星熄灭，有一颗星星亮起。万机之王默默注视着一切，如是演算，日复一日，从不诉诸言语。
t_en: Above the skies, as one star extinguishes, another ignites. The Monarch of Myriad Machines silently watches over all, silently extrapolating day after day, without ever uttering a word.

t_ch: 鉴于「不可知域」存在的风险性，逻辑：有必要为你提供这一区域的演算目标——也即是「学派战争」的相关信息，以提高营救行动的成功率。
t_en: As the Unknowable Domain poses a certain threat, logic: I must provide an extrapolation target for this domain... in other words, information regarding the "Scholars' Strife," to increase the success rate of the rescue mission.

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>「权杖」系统</color>，由鲁珀特二世打造的无机仿生神经元集群，天体级的计算干涉装置，广布在帝国疆土的各个星域，完成帝皇交付的观测、演算与干涉指令。
t_en: The <color=#dbc291ff>Scepter system</color>, inorganic neuron clusters and celestial-level computational interference devices created by Emperor Rubert II, are widely distributed across various quadrants in the imperial domain to complete the observations, extrapolations, and interference orders passed on by him.

t_ch: 第二次帝皇战争结束后，缴获的大量保存完好的「权杖」单元，被星际和平公司转交予博识学会进行研究。
t_en: After the Second Emperor's War, numerous intact Scepter units were handed over to the Intelligentsia Guild by the IPC for research.

t_ch: 但仍未结束的星际能源战争，以及各学派间的嫌隙与野心，却让原本的学术交流逐渐演变成一场以「权杖」系统为中心的<color=#dbc291ff>学派战争。</color>
t_en: However, the active Interstellar Energy War, as well as the animosity and disagreements between the various schools, turned what was originally an academic exchange into a <color=#dbc291ff>Scholars' Strife</color> with the Scepter system at the center.

t_ch: 这次你们的目标是？
t_en: What is your objective this time?

t_ch: 修正表述：是黑塔女士的目标。黑塔女士一直希望能拓宽宇宙的知识总量。而她个人的研究，起于「学派战争」时代留下的{RUBY_B#孤波算法}一道难题{RUBY_E#}。
t_en: Rectified statement: It is Madam Herta's objective. Madam Herta has always hoped to amplify the total knowledge throughout the universe. As for her personal research, it originates from {RUBY_B#solitary waves theory}a conundrum{RUBY_E#} left over from the era of the "Scholars' Strife."

t_ch: 这趟演算，可以说是故地重游，回到「起点」。但她被困在了「起点」，我始料未及。
t_en: It could be said that this extrapolation is a return to the beginning, back to the "origin." However, she has unexpectedly become trapped at this "origin."

t_ch: 你们想研究「权杖」？
t_en: You lot wish to research the Scepters?

t_ch: 对黑塔女士来说，她也许另有目标。黑塔女士一直希望能拓宽宇宙的知识总量。而她个人的研究，起于「学派战争」时代留下的{RUBY_B#孤波算法}一道难题{RUBY_E#}。
t_en: Madam Herta may have another objective. Madam Herta has always hoped to amplify the total knowledge throughout the universe. As for her personal research, it originates from {RUBY_B#solitary waves theory}a conundrum{RUBY_E#} left over from the era of the "Scholars' Strife."

t_ch: 这趟演算，可以说是故地重游，回到「起点」。但她被困在了「起点」，我始料未及。
t_en: It could be said that this extrapolation is a return to the beginning, back to the "origin." However, she has unexpectedly become trapped at this "origin."

t_ch: 希望这些知识会为你的探索提供帮助。
t_en: I hope that this knowledge will assist in your search.

t_ch: 在模拟宇宙的边缘，你终于找到了黑塔熟悉的紫色身影。天才的人偶（其中之一）正站在两段时间的交界地带，尝试剥离「不可知域」的乱码信息。
t_en: At the brink of the Simulated Universe, you finally catch sight of Herta's familiar purple silhouette. The genius puppet (one of them) is currently standing on the boundary between two time periods, trying to peel off the unintelligible information of the Unknowable Domain.

t_ch: 你大声呼唤，但直到走至黑塔的面前，她才恍然发现，模拟宇宙里多出了一个活人。
t_en: You loudly call out to her, but it is not until you are right in front of her that she realizes that the Simulated Universe has gained another living being.

t_ch: 你怎么来了？这里不开放测试，赶紧回其他区域自个儿玩去。
t_en: What are you doing here? This place is not for testing. Go and entertain yourself in some other domains.

t_ch: 黑塔女士，你已与外部失联了<unbreak>34</unbreak>个标准时，{NICKNAME}受我的邀请，专程为寻找你而来。
t_en: Madam Herta, you have been out of contact with the outside world for <unbreak>34</unbreak> universal hours. I specifically requested that {NICKNAME} come to find you.

t_ch: …<unbreak>34</unbreak>个标准时？
t_en: ...<unbreak>34</unbreak> universal hours?

t_ch: 看来「不可知域」不仅会干扰外部的观测，还会影响对时间的感知。
t_en: It seems that not only does the Unknowable Domain interfere with external observation, but it also affects your sense of time.

t_ch: 从外部看是无法观测的乱码，内部居然一切如常。这绝对不是程序错误，它是某种…「脏东西」。
t_en: Though it looks like unobservable nonsense code from outside, everything is completely normal inside. This cannot be a programming error. It is some kind of... "impurity."

t_ch: 黑塔无视你和螺丝咕姆，将目光重新放到眼前的数据流上。你们很快意识到，她并非被困在了「不可知域」，只是——单纯忘了出来。
t_en: Herta ignores you and Screwllum and casts her gaze back towards the data flow before her. Quickly, you realize that it is not that she is trapped in the Unknowable Domain. She simply... forgot to come back.

t_ch: 好吧，这非常黑塔。
t_en: Alright, this is classic Herta.

t_ch: 你们知道<color=#dbc291ff>知识圈</color>吗？
t_en: Do you know about the <color=#dbc291ff>Circle of Knowledge?</color>

t_ch: 黑塔忽地发问。与以往不同，现在的她似乎缺少了些许直爽，正深陷于某个难题的困惑当中。
t_en: Herta asks from nowhere. Unlike before, she doesn't seem as frank and forthright. She is immersed in the confusion of some conundrum before her.

t_ch: 上辈子学过。
t_en: I learned about it in a past life.

t_ch: 完全听不懂。
t_en: I don't understand a word.

t_ch: 我能编一个。
t_en: I can make one up.

t_ch: 把已知的知识比作圆圈，知识越多，圆圈越大，圆圈外的未知也越多。评价：非常巧妙的比喻。
t_en: Comparing one's own knowledge to a circle, the greater your knowledge, the larger your circle, and the more unknowns lay outside your circle. Appraisal: A very astute metaphor.

t_ch: 从我求学开始，每当头脑中知识扩大到一定范围，即将突破已知的圆圈，进入未知的世界时，我就会遭遇某种阻碍。
t_en: Since I began my search for knowledge, whenever the knowledge inside my brain reached a certain level and was about to break through the known circle into the world of the unknown, I would encounter some kind of obstacle.

t_ch: 机器故障、数据污染、乃至人身安全…种种离群概率事件发生，总会把我的研究进展打回原地。
t_en: Mechanical failure, data corruption, even threats to personal safety... All sorts of unlikely events would occur that would push all of my research progress back to zero.

t_ch: 就像有双无形的手，不断阻止我走出已知的圆圈，组成一片连续的「不可知域」。
t_en: It's like there was a pair of invisible hands constantly preventing me from leaving the circle of the known, creating a continuous Unknowable Domain.

t_ch: 逻辑：你似乎在为这些客观的阻碍，赋予某种主观的特质。请容我提醒，主观臆断是研究之敌。
t_en: Logic: You seem to be applying a subjective quality to these objective obstacles. Allow me to remind you, subjectivity is the enemy of research.

t_ch: 对有些人来说是主观臆断，对天才则是灵光一现。我有种直觉，我们在模拟宇宙遇见的这片瓶颈，它在阻止我们获取某些知识，挖掘某段历史……
t_en: To some, it may be subjective conjecture. To a genius, it is a flash of inspiration. My intuition is telling me that this bottleneck of knowledge that we encounter in the Simulated Universe is stopping us from gaining certain knowledge and unearthing a certain period of history...

t_ch: 推论：也许是神秘的「迷思」朝模拟宇宙看了一眼。我枚举了三千二百一十五个推论，想先听哪个？
t_en: Deduction: Perhaps "Mythus" of Enigmata cast their gaze over the Simulated Universe? I have <unbreak>3,215</unbreak> further deductions. Which would you like to hear?

t_ch: 这才是主观臆断吧。
t_en: That sounds more like subjective conjecture to me.

t_ch: 出去再聊吧。
t_en: Let's talk about this outside.

t_ch: 溜了溜了。
t_en: I'm out of here.

t_ch: 黑塔罕见地沉默下来，她望向「不可知域」的深处，没有挪动脚步。仿佛那里有何物正等待着她，如一道谜题，呼唤天才的答案。
t_en: Herta falls into a moment of uncharacteristic silence. She stands perfectly still and stares into the depths of the Unknowable Domain. It's as if something is waiting there for her. Like a puzzle that calls for a genius to solve it.

t_ch: 算了，下次再来也不迟。
t_en: Forget it. I can always come back.

t_ch: 一串代码以超乎常理的方式钻出装置，它的数据凝固，化作熟悉的形象。
t_en: A string of code worms out of the device in a most extraordinary manner, its data coalescing into a most familiar image.

t_ch: 「呼…终于进来了。应该没被寂静领主发现吧…」斯蒂芬•劳艾德警惕地四处张望。
t_en: "Phew... Finally in. The Lord of Silence probably didn't notice me..." Stephen Lloyd looks around warily.

t_ch: 好酷的出场！
t_en: What a cool entrance!

t_ch: 能合个影吗？
t_en: Can I get a selfie?

t_ch: 「那个…额，我重新构建了一套演算模型，从寂静领主那儿抢回了部分权限。」斯蒂芬•劳艾德生硬地转移话题。
t_en: "Err... I reconstructed an extrapolation model and wrested back partial permissions from the Lord of Silence." Stephen Lloyd stiffly changes the subject.

t_ch: 「在玩家对战模式里作弊，简直是对游戏的亵渎！」他努力做出愤慨的表情，但气势仍带着一丝怯弱。
t_en: "Cheating in PvP is a blasphemy to games!" He tries his best to appear angry, but his poise is somewhat meek.

t_ch: 你头一次见他这么激动，甚至短暂性克服了群体恐惧。被波尔卡屏蔽的<color=#dbc291ff>「权杖」系统重新上线</color>。但这微小的波动在寂静领主的观测中犹如惊雷。
t_en: This is the first time you've seen him so agitated. He even temporarily overcame his social anxiety. The <color=#dbc291ff>Scepter system that Polka blocked comes online once more</color>, but this minute ripple pings the Lord of Silence's observations like a thundering shock.

t_ch: 斯蒂芬掏出*频变捕手*，试图隐藏自己的踪迹。糖果色的影子现身，从斯蒂芬原先站立的位置出现，凝聚成像，如同蝴蝶钻破蛹壳般碾碎了斯蒂芬的数据。
t_en: Stephen whips out his "frequency scrambler" in an attempt to conceal his tracks. A candy-colored shadow spawns, appearing where Stephen was standing, manifesting into a figure, grinding Stephen's data into dust like a butterfly breaking out of its chrysalis shell.

t_ch: 「我先先先先撤啦——」斯蒂芬的残留声音也一同消散。
t_en: "I'm g-g-g-g-going to go first..." Stephen's fading echo also dissipates into the air.

t_ch: 你嗅到熟悉的糕点香氛，点缀着惊惶鹿的花朵。她悠闲地站在模拟实验室里，仿佛打一开始就知道，你们会从这里经过。
t_en: You catch a whiff of a familiar confectionery scent adorned with Startled Deer flowers. She is idly standing in the simulated lab as if she knew from the beginning that you would pass by here.

t_ch: 原来惊喜在这儿！
t_en: So the surprise is here!

t_ch: 终于等到你了。
t_en: You're finally here.

t_ch: 你很难得看到阮•梅的嘴角没有带上那抹笑意，「看起来，你们为模拟宇宙找来了一位不得了的舞伴。」
t_en: It is rare for you to see Ruan Mei without a smile curled at the corners of her lips, "It appears that you've found a terrifying dance partner for the Simulated Universe."

t_ch: 她将糕点徐徐吞下，「不过，即便是寂静领主也不会想到，底层代码里还藏着一段微型切片。」
t_en: She nibbles on the cake slowly, "But even the Lord of Silence won't guess that a minuscule slice is hiding in the system's root."

t_ch: 「我还以为你不会来了。」
t_en: "I thought you weren't coming."

t_ch: 「你们两个的事情，我向来很关心。」阮•梅的手指搭在实验台上，细微的震动预示飓风将至。她不紧不慢地敞开窗，欣赏那道迫近的飓风。
t_en: "I've always been concerned about affairs involving you two." Ruan Mei's fingers rest on the lab bench, their subtle vibrations heralding the advent of a hurricane. She leisurely opens the window, admiring the furious gales raiding near.

t_ch: 「螺丝咕姆让我转告你们，{RUBY_B#核}饵{RUBY_E#}已经布好，只待猎物上钩。相信你们能读懂这晦涩的比喻。」
t_en: "Screwllum asked me to send you a message: The {RUBY_B#core}bait{RUBY_E#} has been laid, and we're just waiting for the prey to bite. I trust you all comprehend this obscure metaphor."

t_ch: 「在离开前，我会给你们一点厉害的东西，像平时那样。」
t_en: "Before leaving, I will give you all something quite powerful, just like how I usually do."

t_ch: 「祝你们好运。」阮•梅将你们移出切片，在「死亡」抵达的前一秒消失不见。
t_en: "Good luck." Ruan Mei transports you all out of the section, disappearing the second before "Death" arrives.

t_ch: 「0％的完整度，看来是停在核心瘫痪后的节点了。」黑塔皱皱眉头，「喂喂，螺丝咕姆，能听到吗？快来处理下。」
t_en: "0％ complete — looks like it's stopped at the node after the core was disabled." Herta frowns. "Hello, Screwllum, do you read? Hurry up and fix this."

t_ch: 「『不可知域』的乱流正在干扰你们所在的登陆区域，从外部无法锁定具体的时间节点。」螺丝咕姆的声音悠悠传来，「提议：对模拟宇宙的记录进行整体回溯，回到『权杖』最完整的时间，并接入核心。」
t_en: "The Unknowable Domain's turbulence is interfering with your current landing area — there is no way to lock in on the exact time period." Screwllum's voice is nonchalantly transmitted. "Suggestion: Perform complete backtrack on the Simulated Universe's records, returning to the Scepter's most complete moment, and connect to the core."

t_ch: 「怎么弄都行，赶紧开始吧。」
t_en: "Do whatever is required. Just get it started."

t_ch: 一颗星星迎面向你们撞来。你认出它悲郁的色彩，鲁珀特二世的衣冠冢，帕提维娅疯狂地在墓碑上刻印出公式，铁皮和着血污嵌进手掌。
t_en: A star hurtles towards you all. You recognize its melancholy hue — Rubert II's cenotaph, Patavia frantically carving her formula on the tombstone, iron flakes and bloodstains etched into her palms.

t_ch: 横冲直撞的航船，在时间的湍流中溯源而上。你们越过烟尘弥漫的银河图书馆，地面上蚂蚁般的人群嘶吼，「将知识归还给知识！」
t_en: The rampaging ship sails upstream along the deluge of time. You traverse the dust-mired Cosmic Library, the crowds on the ground congregating like ants, screaming "Return knowledge to knowledge!"

t_ch: 回溯。
t_en: Backtrack.

t_ch: 火光穿过层层锈蚀的金属，那里站着千千万万个梅尼科，有机喉咙里迸发出冰冷的指令，「开始启动反有机方程演算。」
t_en: The blazing ray penetrates layers of rusted metal, tens of millions of Meniks standing there, frigid commands erupting from organic throats: "Activate Anti-Organic Equation extrapolation."

t_ch: 继续回溯。
t_en: Continue backtracking.

t_ch: 时间倒退，为你们披上学者的衣装，未来学大会上人声鼎沸，学士们相互推搡，从身旁穿行而过。帕提维娅在同谁人交谈，面带微笑，「未来？呵，未来。」
t_en: Time rewinds, shrouding you all with scholars' attires. At the Futurological Congress, the roiling voices of a thronging crowd accompanied by scholars jostling with one another and pushing past you. Patavia is talking to someone, her face covered in a mild smile, "The future? Heh, the future."

t_ch: 继续回溯！
t_en: Continue backtracking!

t_ch: 「何必匆匆向前呢，各位朋友。」倒行的人群仿佛接到了语声的命令，光阴在刹那间被冻结。帕提维娅成了动弹不得的冰雕，而她身边的女人——那个本该是虚假数据体的女人，将目光投向你们。
t_en: "Why in such haste to advance forth, my friends?" Time freezes in that instant as if the crowd moving in reverse hears a commanding voice. Patavia becomes an immobile statue of ice and the woman beside her — the woman who should have been an entity composed of false data — turns her gaze to you.

t_ch: 「黑塔，你和你的朋友们组织起的这场偷窥过去秘密的小游戏该结束了。在我的手术刀切断它之前，要不要同我跳上一支舞？」她脱下学士服，糖果色的纱裙摇曳，看不清面庞。
t_en: "Herta, it's time to stop this little game that you and your friends have concocted to peek at past secrets. How about a dance with me before I sever it with my scalpel?" She takes off her scholars' uniform, her candy-colored gown sashaying, and her face unclear.

t_ch: 「检测到…」通讯中断，模拟宇宙的回溯进程骤然崩溃。
t_en: "Detected..." The communication breaks off and the Simulated Universe's backtracking progress suddenly collapses.

t_ch: 「我感受到有人在窥探我的<color=#dbc291ff>足迹</color>。」女人掏出手术刀，放到手中仔细打磨。「于是我为他们准备了回礼，一场猫捉老鼠的游戏。」
t_en: "I can feel someone prying into my <color=#dbc291ff>steps</color>." The woman takes out a scalpel, places it in her palm, and starts polishing it. "So I am preparing a gift in return — a game of cat and mouse."

t_ch: 检测到非法登录，防火墙阻拦失败，「不可知域」权限更改，禁止任何弹出申请，访客申请验证……
t_en: Illegal login detected. Firewall interception failed. Unknowable Domain permissions changed. All requests blocked, verifying visitor request...

t_ch: 身份验证完成，天才俱乐部#4……
t_en: Identity verification complete. Genius Society #4...

t_ch: 「还愣着干什么，{NICKNAME}，跑！」
t_en: "What're you standing there for, {NICKNAME}? Run!"

t_ch: 「欢迎来到『全知域』，我精心打造的实验场。」
t_en: "Welcome to the Knowable Domain, the experimental environment that I meticulously created."

t_ch: 想象一只蝴蝶，一只微不足道，振翅飞翔的蝴蝶。
t_en: Imagine a butterfly, an insignificant butterfly flitting its wings.

t_ch: 在伊斯特邦联的一场比赛中，它被选手的挥拍击中。鳞羽上的粉尘引发了呼吸道过敏，又让喷嚏连连的选手将微不足道的感冒病毒传染给了接见他的总统。
t_en: In an Isthmian Confederation competition, it was struck by a competitor's bat. The powder from its wings triggered a respiration allergy in the competitor, causing him to sneeze non-stop. Later, that competitor passed on a trifling cold virus to his president whom he was visiting.

t_ch: 感冒带来的头疼脑热令决策树向「攻击性」的象限大胆偏斜了百分之二，最终总统签署命令，向邻邦发动战争，无数人因此丧生。
t_en: The headache and fever brought about by the cold caused the decision tree to boldly tilt towards the "aggression" quadrant by two percent. Eventually, the president signed an order to declare war on their neighboring confederation, resulting in countless deaths.

t_ch: 总统的私人医生听闻爱人丧命战场的消息，她用一柄手术刀切开了总统如今无恙的呼吸道，绝望地将凶器从高处抛落。
t_en: The president's private physician received news that her lover was killed in the war. She used a scalpel to slice open the president's now-recovered throat, and despairingly disposed of the murder weapon from a high vantage point.

t_ch: 路过的杀手将从天而降的凶器——这支足以钉住掀起风暴的蝴蝶的「标本针」拾起，紧握在手。
t_en: The passing killer picked up this murder weapon that fell from the sky — this specimen needle capable of nailing a butterfly that could whip up storms — and tightly gripped it in her hand.

t_ch: 这名杀手以天才为目标，她的手术刀即将逼近你们的脖颈。而现在，你们面前有三个避难所。为躲过追捕，挣扎的猎物要逃往何方？
t_en: This killer regards geniuses as targets, her scalpel closing in on your necks. And now, three shelters lie before you. To avoid capture, where will the struggling prey flee to?

t_ch: 中子星堡垒-存活率<unbreak>99.99</unbreak>％。
t_en: Neutron Star Fortress - probability of survival: <unbreak>99.99</unbreak>％.

t_ch: 一只蝴蝶扇动翅膀，鳞羽上的粒子恰到好处地发生反应。它掉进偶然形成的微型虫洞，因巧合在中子星的内部绽放，令严丝合缝的宅邸崩裂开。
t_en: When a butterfly flaps its wings, the particles on its scaled wings conjure a reaction in just the right amounts. It falls into a micro-wormhole that randomly forms, blooming from a coincidence within a neutron star, tearing apart a perfectly sealed residence.

t_ch: 钢铁监牢-存活率<unbreak>57.02</unbreak>％。
t_en: Iron Prison - probability of survival: <unbreak>57.02</unbreak>％.

t_ch: 一只蝴蝶扇动翅膀，鳞羽上的粒子恰到好处地发生反应。它飘进防护网，意外干扰到某段维护控温系统的内部信号，将监牢内的空间变作火海。
t_en: When a butterfly flaps its wings, the particles on its scaled wings conjure a reaction in just the right amounts. It drifts into the safety net, accidentally disrupting some internal signal within the temperature control maintenance system, sending the space inside the prison into a sea of fire.

t_ch: 水晶旅店-存活率<unbreak>8.58</unbreak>％。
t_en: Crystal Hotel - probability of survival: <unbreak>8.58</unbreak>％.

t_ch: 一只蝴蝶扇动翅膀，鳞羽上的粒子恰到好处地发生反应。它飘落的轨迹完美地与引力契合，越来越多的灰尘包裹住它，直到一颗滚石将旅店压扁。
t_en: When a butterfly flaps its wings, the particles on its scaled wings conjure a reaction in just the right amounts. Its drifting, downward descent acts in perfect tandem with gravity, more and more dust wrapping around it, till it becomes a rolling rock that crushes the hotel.

t_ch: 「在『全知域』，概率和巧合不具备意义。」波尔卡平静地预言，「你们会迎来一场笑剧，而后是一出悲剧。」
t_en: "In the Knowable Domain, probability and coincidences bear no meaning." Polka calmly foretells. "You will welcome a play of comedy, and then one of tragedy."

t_ch: 紊乱的数据阻断了模拟宇宙对「权杖」系统的演算，<color=#dbc291ff>你再也连接不上「权杖」。</color>
t_en: The turbulent data hinders the Simulated Universe's extrapolation on the Scepter system — <color=#dbc291ff>you are unable to reconnect to the Scepter.</color>

t_ch: 曾有一种信条，将世界视为一台精密的发条钟表，倘若掌握每颗粒子的信息，便能预测出所有运动的轨迹。也即，未来已经被过去所决定。虽然这一想法不过是痴人说梦，但在此之外，宇宙中却存在更为直观的参考系——由博识尊演算出的「时刻」。
t_en: There was once a belief to view the world as a meticulous clockwork piece, where if information about every atom is grasped, then all movements' trajectories could be predicted. Simply put, the future is already determined by the past. While this concept is no more than words spoken in a fool's dream, a more intuitive frame of reference exists in the universe beyond — The concept of "Instant" as extrapolated by Nous.

t_ch: 「我收到了一张邀请函，以鲁珀特的复活作为封蜡。」声音保持着距离，却随时能将你们置于死地，「…以第五位天才加入你们可笑的项目为诱饵。」
t_en: "I received an invitation with Rubert's resurrection as its seal." The voice keeps its distance but is easily capable of ending you at any moment. "...with the fifth genius joining your laughable project as bait."

t_ch: 「所以，我来了。但不是为加入你们而来……」
t_en: "So, I am here. But 'tis not to join you all..."

t_ch: 一个人的死亡，成为帝皇战争的导火索。一个变量的疏漏，反有机病毒将学派战争推向崩溃的边缘。一颗粒子的变化，爆炸湮灭了改变世界的灵感。只要施加一个微小的变量，未来就会截然不同，宇宙的面貌将幡然变改，群星将成为造物大锅中的混沌汤。
t_en: The death of one person becomes the spark for the Emperor's War. The oversight of one variable means the Anti-Organic Virus pushed the Scholars' Strife to the brink of destruction. The change in one particle causes an explosion that obliterates an inspiration to change the world. As long as an infinitesimal variable is added, the future would become vastly different, the face of the universe becomes radically altered, and the stars turn into a chaotic soup in the great cauldron of creation.

t_ch: 如果圆圈外未知的知识会破坏确定的未来，那么，让它们不再出现便好。在未来模型被导向不可知前，有一位天才立于黑暗中，推落第一块骨牌，以天才的尸骨铸成的骨牌。
t_en: If unknown knowledge outside the circle threatens to disrupt the ascertained future, then such knowledge should never appear again. Before the future model can be guided into the unknown, a genius stands in the darkness, pushing down the first domino — a domino set forged from the bones of geniuses.

t_ch: 「我来，是为杀死你们会带来的种种混沌可能性。」
t_en: "I come to kill the myriad of chaotic possibilities you will bring."

t_ch: 「关于寂静领主一意追杀天才，我们几个猜想过一千种理由…为嫉妒？为权势？为掠夺知识？我们唯独没想到这个——熄灭混沌的可能性？你的犯罪动机还真是别具一格啊。」
t_en: "We have speculated over a thousand reasons regarding the Lord of Silence's relentless hunt for geniuses... Envy? Power? Or the plundering of knowledge? This is the only reason we hadn't considered — Extinguishing chaotic possibilities? Your motive for your crimes is truly unique."

t_ch: 「你，杀死天才，试图限制宇宙知识的增长，是为了把整个宇宙都变成自己的『全知域』吗？」
t_en: "You murder geniuses in an attempt to stem the growth of cosmic knowledge... Is it just so you can turn the entire universe into your own Knowable Domain?"

t_ch: 难道说，黑塔遇到的那些意外？
t_en: You mean... those accidents that Herta encountered...?

t_ch: 难道说，学派战争的背后也是？
t_en: You mean... the one behind the Scholars' Strife as well?

t_ch: 寂静领主沉默不语。她不屑回应你们的指控。
t_en: The Lord of Silence does not speak. She does not deign to respond to your accusations.

t_ch: 一只蝴蝶扇动翅膀，哪怕数百光年外的世界因此毁灭，蝴蝶也对此一无所知，因为它只是在扇动翅膀。为实验修正偏离的变量，避免未知信息的干扰，这是每位学者都会做的事。
t_en: When a butterfly beats its wings, even if worlds hundreds of light-years away are destroyed because of this, the butterfly would not know any better because it is just beating its wings. Correcting deviating variables in an experiment and avoiding interference from unknown information — this is what every scholar would do.

t_ch: 这是诡辩！
t_en: This is just sophistry!

t_ch: 这是推卸责任！
t_en: This is just irresponsible!

t_ch: 「倘若用思维实验将宇宙关进圆圈，就会成为罪人。那为宇宙锚定『时刻』，把所有智慧都关进『智识』的祂，又要如何审判？」她并非在询问，只是自说自话。
t_en: "If one would be considered a sinner to use a thought experiment to confine the universe in a circle, then how are we to judge THEM, who anchors 'Instants' onto the universe and locks all wisdom into 'Erudition'?" She is not inquiring. She merely talks to herself.

t_ch: 「难道…你在替祂执行指令？！不，这不符合祂「求知」的神性。」
t_en: "Are you... executing THEIR orders!? No, this does not match THEIR divine trait of seeking knowledge."

t_ch: 「你有没有想过，『完美的计算』也是一种求知——你很清楚，没人能违背祂的计算。」
t_en: "Have you ever considered that 'Perfect Calculation' is also a form of seeking knowledge? You should be very aware that no one can defy THEIR calculations."

t_ch: 「如果我连通鲁珀特二世留下的所有『权杖』进行思考，就像那位帕提维娅……创造出一个<color=#dbc291ff>知识奇点</color>呢？我是说，既然祂如你说的那般，要将宇宙限定在完美的枷锁里，又何必准许帝皇造出那些用于思考的『天体神经元』…那些『权杖』，准许他跨出知识的圆圈？」
t_en: "If I connect to all the Scepters that Rubert II left behind and start to think, just like how that Patavia... How she created a <color=#dbc291ff>Knowledge Singularity</color>? What I'm saying is, if THEY are going to confine the universe within the fetters of perfection as you claim, why would the emperor have been allowed to create those 'Celestial-Body Neurons' used for thought... Did those Scepters allow him to step beyond the circle of knowledge?"

t_ch: 「……」她在思考黑塔的提问吗？还是说，仅是在思考，怎样富有趣味地杀死你们？
t_en: "..." Is she contemplating Herta's question? Or is she merely thinking about how to kill you all in the most entertaining way?

t_ch: 「问得好，你比其他的受害者更敏锐。」面目模糊的女人声音听来如此悲伤，「人的思考仅限于方寸头颅之内，而神明的思考，以星光明灭为神经元放电…以我等天才对垒的胜负为思考结论。」
t_en: "Good question. You're sharper than the rest of the victims." The murky-faced woman's voice sounds sorrowful. "Human thoughts are processed within our tiny brains, while divine thoughts are on a scale where the birth and implosion of stars are mere sparks on THEIR neurons... And the conclusion of THEIR thoughts is nothing less than the outcome of us geniuses' battles to the death."

t_ch: 天才们，「智识」延伸进现实的思维突触，星神思考的具象化展现。
t_en: Geniuses. Thought synapses that the Erudition extends into reality. The physical manifestation and phenomenon of Aeonic thought.

t_ch: 「祂用『时刻』宣告了完美的未来模型。而你，是一只挣脱昆虫针的蝴蝶标本，你扇动翅翼掀起混沌和未知，这会提前将宇宙导向『终末』。」
t_en: "THEY used 'Instants' to declare a perfect model of the future, and you are a butterfly specimen that escaped from its needle. You flap your wings and roil up the chaos and the unknown — this will prematurely lead the universe to Finality."

t_ch: 「我不允许。我会将你们一个、一个钉死在这『全知域』中。」她叹息，像是在为即将被制成标本的蝴蝶的脆弱之美而叹息。
t_en: "I won't allow it. I will stake you down with nails, one by one, inside this Knowable Domain." She sighs, as if lamenting the fragile beauty of a butterfly about to be turned into a specimen.

t_ch: 「好大的口气。你应该知道这是我们主动发出的邀请，对吧？」
t_en: "A bold statement. You do know that we were the ones who sent out the invite, don't you?"

t_ch: 「我看到了，我看到祂的演算了，」波尔卡的语调冰冷，「一个『时刻』，<color=#dbc291ff>一个天才们挣扎的『时刻』</color>来了。黑塔、斯蒂芬•劳艾德、阮•梅、螺丝咕姆…还有你，误入网中的虫儿，尽你们所能，挣扎吧！」她将破碎的面部转向你，你的直觉一阵毛骨悚然，她在微笑。
t_en: "I see it. I see THEIR extrapolations." Polka's tone is chilling. "An 'Instant,' <color=#dbc291ff>an 'Instant' where geniuses shall struggle</color> has arrived. Herta, Stephen Lloyd, Ruan Mei, Screwllum... and you, a little bug who stumbled into a web. Struggle with all your might!" She turns her shattered face towards you. Your instincts send your entire body into a hair-standing frenzy — She is smiling.

t_ch: 帝皇的衣冠冢，自我加冕的终点。不巧的是，有人比你们来得更早。挣扎的瞬间，定格在希望破灭的刹那，将成为蝴蝶最美丽的时刻。
t_en: The Emperor's cenotaph, the end of self-coronation. Unfortunately, others came earlier than you. That flash of struggle, suspended in that instant of hope's decimation — Will become the butterfly's most beautiful moment.

t_ch: 「真是恶趣味。」
t_en: "You sure have a twisted sense of what you find interesting."

t_ch: 那道孤波，同样的研究对象，两位天才却得出了两种同样正确，却水火不容的理论——其中一位说，宇宙不是发条玩具，未来永远悬而未定。而另一位却说……
t_en: With that same solitary wave as the research topic, two geniuses obtained equally correct but completely divergent theories — One of them said the universe isn't a clockwork toy, that the future is forever in an unknown pendency. And the other one said...

t_ch: 「宇宙是一轮精密的钟表，现在，天才们停止挣扎的『时刻』就要到了。」波尔卡擦拭着手术刀，缓步向你们逼近。
t_en: "The universe is an exquisitely precise clock. Now, the 'Instant' where the geniuses stop struggling is about to arrive." Polka polishes her scalpel, slowly sauntering towards you.

t_ch: 「结束？嗝，老子还没冲出宇宙呢！」一艘支离破碎的贡多拉撞进花海，从中跌出的却是一名头戴假面的小丑。远方的骑士高声赞颂：「何等纯美的壮举！被模拟的生命打算向真实进军！」
t_en: "Over? Hic, I haven't even charged out of the universe!" A shattered gondola crashes into the sea of flowers and a masked clown stumbles out of it. The knight from afar extols resoundingly: "A stance of utter beauty! Life that is being simulated intends to assault the truth!"

t_ch: 「我已经再三强调，我不是虚构出来的！」他的身旁，一名虚构史学家怒吼。
t_en: "I've already told you umpteenth times! I'm not fictional!" A History Fictionologist yells out angrily beside him.

t_ch: 「……」寂静领主沉默无言，你无法得知这重如天体的沉默是出自困惑，还是隐而不发的愤怒。
t_en: "..." The Lord of Silence responds with silence. You cannot ascertain if that divine-esque silence stems from confusion, or a simmering, seething rage.

t_ch: 数百米高的巨大铁人踏碎了模拟宇宙的天空球，一只脚踏起了山摇地动。筑城者用巨大的扩音器高喊，「虚假的壁垒，亦是琥珀王对我的试炼！」
t_en: An iron giant hundreds of meters tall crushes the Simulated Universe's skybox, each step sending shockwaves across the ground. Through the loudspeaker, the Architects bellow: "The false bastion is a trial meted unto me by the Amber Lord!"

t_ch: 你们团建来了？
t_en: You guys on a team building exercise?

t_ch: 还有高手？
t_en: Another master has appeared?

t_ch: 无数个角落里，数据如暴雨和狂风钻出：厄兆先锋的铃声，悲悼伶人的哭泣，焚化工点燃喷火器的热浪——它们只是这个宇宙中安分守己的代码，但在这一刻却一齐涌现。
t_en: From countless corners, data erupts like torrential rain and howling winds: The chime of the Omen Vanguards, the sorrowful wails of the Mourning Actors, and the scorching heat from The Cremators' flamethrowers — these were just compliant lines of code within this universe, but at this moment, they all surge forth.

t_ch: 模拟宇宙，疯了。这并非代码错误导致的混乱，而是天才们精心设下的戏码。调用，复制，调用，复制……宇宙图景释放了所有资源，试图用疯狂将迫近的「全知杀手」挤出舞台。万物仿佛高喊着，此处没有你的立足之地，波尔卡！
t_en: The Simulated Universe descends into madness. This chaos isn't caused by coding errors, but a carefully orchestrated plan by the geniuses. Call, copy, call, copy... The cosmic scene deploys all its resources, trying to force the approaching "Omniscient Killer" off the stage with chaos. Everything seems to scream, "There's no place for you here, Polka!"

t_ch: 「为我准备的陷阱么…」糖果色的杀手扼住风暴的咽喉，手术刀切、刺、划……代码尽数化为支离的碎片。她举手投足，高视阔步，目光所及之下一切观测系成了僵死凝固的铁板，回归井然有序。
t_en: "A trap prepared just for me...?" The candy-colored killer grips the storm by its throat, using her scalpel to slice, stab, and cut... The code is shattered into pieces. With each movement, her gaze freezes everything in sight into a rigid iron panel, restoring order to the chaos.

t_ch: 「现在，你们还有什么把戏可玩？」波尔卡姿态闲适，面向你。尽管你的视线无法穿透遮覆其五官的面部认知滤网，但她轻轻的哼笑似乎出卖了心情。
t_en: "Now, what other tricks do you have up your sleeve?" Polka faces you with a relaxed posture. Although you cannot penetrate the facial recognition filter that's hiding her features, her muffled scoff seems to betray her mood.

t_ch: 「在我面前，扑克的牌张顺序没有随机性可言，硬币的正反面是预设好的结果。风暴也好，波涛也罢，一切混沌要按我要求的方向转动。」
t_en: "In my presence, the sequence of poker cards is not random, and the outcome of a coin flip has been predetermined. Whether it's storm or waves, all chaos shall move according to my instructions."

t_ch: 她笑了起来。「哼…哈哈……哼哼哈哈哈哈？」这到底有什么可笑的？她的笑声中尽是疑惑，让你也摸不着头脑。
t_en: She starts laughing. "Hoo... Haha... Hoohoohahaha?" What's so funny about this? There is doubt in her laughter, confusing you as well.

t_ch: 焦虑和恐惧被一阵突如其来欢欣之风焚尽。亢奋、癫狂、无上的愉悦攫住你的感官。模拟宇宙的天幕上响起一声惊雷般清脆的声音——
t_en: Anxiety and fear are burned away by a sudden gust of joyous wind. Exhilaration, madness, and unparalleled delight grip your senses. A crisp and thunderous sound rings through the sky of the Simulated Universe.

t_ch: <size=36>「阿哈！」</size>
t_en: <size=36>"Aha!"</size>

t_ch: 你大笑，在场的所有人，所有代码，万事万物开始大笑。
t_en: You burst into laughter, and soon everyone present, every line of code, and all things begin laughing.

t_ch: 「阿基维利，你又惹了什么麻烦？」如同一团面具的疯狂之源自群星中现身，向你行了个礼，「瞧瞧这个地方，所有东西都被固定在轨道上，简直安静得可怕。要我说，星星们都该哈哈大笑，毕竟，无论是这个假宇宙，还是那个真的，都不过是个拙劣的笑话。」
t_en: "Akivili, what trouble did you get yourself into this time?" The source of madness in the guise of a cluster of masks appears and gives you a bow. "Take a look at this place. Everything is locked in its path, and it's eerily silent. Honestly, the stars should all burst into laughter, because whether it's this false universe or the real one, they're both just poorly executed jokes."

t_ch: 阿哈，我真想死你了！
t_en: Aha, I really missed you!

t_ch: 「我也好想你啊，想得头都弄丢了。」
t_en: "I missed you too, so much that I've lost my head!"

t_ch: 谁把这鬼东西放出来的？
t_en: Who let this ghastly thing out?

t_ch: 「一只黄色的小鸭子告诉我，多出来逛逛，有利于身心健康。」
t_en: "A little yellow duckling once told me, 'go out and touch some grass. That's good for your health.'"

t_ch: 「逻辑：孤波算法，模拟宇宙的演算基础，当系统内的不稳定程度超出界限，任何运动都会变得无法预测。」你听到他的齿轮转动，「我改写了系统的倾向性，告知他们世界的真相，我为波尔卡女士的模型，带来了一只混沌的蝴蝶。」
t_en: "Logic: The solitary waves theory is the extrapolation fundamental to the Simulated Universe. Once the system's extent of instability hits its limit, all motions become unpredictable." You hear his gears churning. "I've rewritten the system's tendencies, revealing the truth of the world to them. I've introduced a chaotic butterfly into Madam Polka's model."

t_ch: 「干得漂亮，螺丝咕姆！我头一次觉得这家伙的话那么悦耳。」
t_en: "Nicely done, Screwllum! This is my first time hearing such pleasing words from this fella."

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>阿哈宣告，宇宙如疯人的玩笑，混沌痴愚，不可洞知。</color>「全知域」自此更名「不可知域」。
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>Aha declares, the universe is like a madman's jest — chaotic, irrational, and beyond comprehension. </color>The Knowable Domain will henceforth be known as the Unknowable Domain.

t_ch: 一切事工已毕，祂说——
t_en: THEY rested from all the work of creating that THEY had done, and said...

t_ch: <size=36>「要有笑声。」</size>
t_en: <size=36>"Let there be laughter."</size>

t_ch: 宇宙在混乱中加速膨胀，如同小丑在生日宴会上为孩子们吹胀的气球。无论是你们，还是波尔卡，彼此的距离都在膨胀中被无限延长。你看见黑塔走进核心，全宇宙的「权杖」在共鸣中再度成为完整的神经系统。
t_en: The universe rapidly expands in the midst of the chaos, like how a clown blows up balloons at a children's birthday party. Whether it's you or Polka, the distance between you both stretches endlessly. You watch Herta walk into the core, all of the Scepters in the universe in complete resonance, reuniting into one neural system.

t_ch: 「寂静领主的目标是要阻止我们走出知识圆圈。那么，她的出现，是否意味着我们已身处圆圈边缘？」
t_en: "The goal of the Lord of Silence is to stop us from leaving the Circle of Knowledge. Then, with her appearance, does it mean we are currently at the edge of the circle?"

t_ch: 「你会像所有的天才那般，永远无法知晓答案了。」波尔卡并不慌张，她整理自己糖果色的衣袍，如同拨动蝴蝶的鳞羽，在宇宙中掀起最后的飓风。未来是确定的还是混沌的？学派战争的难题在二人身上重演。
t_en: "You'll be like all the other geniuses, forever unable to know the answer." Polka remains unfazed, tidying her candy-colored robe, like how a butterfly flutters its wings, unleashing the final cyclone upon the universe. Is the future certain or chaotic? The conundrum of the Scholars' Strife plays out once again between the two.

t_ch: 「我们会有很多共同语言，不是吗？」黑塔笑了，「波尔卡，要不要加入我们？」
t_en: "We'll have a lot in common to talk about, no?" Herta smiles. "Polka, would you like to join us?"

t_ch: 「我拒绝。我们确信心中的猜想是唯一正确的猜想，这一点不会改变。」
t_en: "I refuse. We both are certain that the hypothesis in our mind is the only correct one, and that certainty will not waver."

t_ch: 女人看向天空，她那无形的视线宛如钉死蝴蝶的标本针，将某个结果确凿无疑地钉死在了模拟宇宙中——天空中星罗棋布的「权杖」开始瘫痪熄火。
t_en: The woman looks skyward, her invisible gaze like an insect pin that stakes down butterflies, pinning down a certain outcome in the Simulated Universe without the shadow of a doubt — The Scepters scattered among the sky begin to malfunction and extinguish.

t_ch: 「也正因如此，我们成了天才。」
t_en: "And that is how we became geniuses."

t_ch: 你俩要不关心关心我？
t_en: Can the both of you show me some concern?

t_ch: 风太大，我听不清。
t_en: The wind is too loud, and I can't hear you.

t_ch: 在狂乱的宇宙风中，两位天才的对垒一瞬间就落下了帷幕。
t_en: In the tempestuous cosmic winds, the battle between the two geniuses comes to an immediate end.

t_ch: 「『权杖』…全完了！我还没像帕提维娅那样…提出我的问题啊！」她在狂风中跺脚。
t_en: "The Scepters... It's all over! I haven't even gotten the chance to ask my question... like Patavia did!" She stomps her foot in rage.

t_ch: 「你为我献上了一幕精彩的舞剧，我就用你剩下的生命作为小费回报，」女人再次整了整糖果色衣袍的褶皱，仿佛她极其在意其上每一道纹路是否各安其位。「就此别过了，小家伙，你该庆幸自己依旧停留在『知识圆圈』里。」
t_en: "You've given me a spectacular performance, and I will grant you the rest of your life as my tip." The woman adjusts her candy-colored robe as if the position of each crease on the fabric is of utmost importance. "Farewell, little one. Count your lucky stars that you're still within the Circle of Knowledge."

t_ch: 宇宙停止了暴胀，那抹糖果色的身影溃散在空无中，把巨大的沉默抛给了你和黑塔。你迟疑半晌，决定开口。
t_en: The universe stops its expansion, and that candy-colored figure dissolves into emptiness, leaving a profound silence hanging over you and Herta. After a moment of hesitation, you decide to break the silence.

t_ch: 这次模拟演算失败了？
t_en: This simulated extrapolation failed?

t_ch: 「怎么会，我们成功邀请了第五位天才加入模拟宇宙项目——她拒绝了。知道答案就够了。」
t_en: "Of course not! We've successfully invited the fifth genius to collaborate on the Simulated Universe project — but she declined. Knowing the answer is enough."

t_ch: 我们击败了波尔卡？
t_en: We've defeated Polka?

t_ch: 「……不愧是天才们的杀手。只是一次现身，就几乎让整个项目瘫痪报废。」
t_en: "...A real killer of geniuses indeed. With just one appearance, she nearly brought the entire project to a grinding halt."

t_ch: 「好在也不算一无所获。我们成功邀请了第五位天才加入模拟宇宙项目——她拒绝了。唉，知道这个答案就够了。」
t_en: "Luckily, it was not entirely in vain. We've successfully invited the fifth genius to collaborate on the Simulated Universe project — but she declined. *sigh* Knowing this answer is enough."

t_ch: 黑塔？你还好吗？
t_en: Herta? Are you okay?

t_ch: 「……」你一度以为她的人偶停止了运作。
t_en: "..." You temporarily believe that her puppet has stopped working.

t_ch: 「……不愧是天才们的杀手。只是一次现身，就几乎让整个项目瘫痪报废。」
t_en: "...A real killer of geniuses indeed. With just one appearance, she nearly brought the entire project to a grinding halt."

t_ch: 「好在也不算一无所获。我们成功邀请了第五位天才加入模拟宇宙项目——她拒绝了。唉，知道答案就够了。」
t_en: "Luckily, it was not entirely in vain. We've successfully invited the fifth genius to collaborate on the Simulated Universe project — but she declined. *sigh* Knowing this answer is enough."

t_ch: 波尔卡离去了。你松了口气，你们捡回一命。
t_en: Polka has left. You relax. Your lives are safe.

t_ch: 「真想扒下这家伙的面部认知滤网，把她的照片传到星际和平网络最显眼的版块。」人偶伸了个懒腰——这一定是她身为人类时的习惯。
t_en: "I really want to rip off that facial recognition filter and post her picture on the most obvious spot on the main page of the Interastral Peace Network." The doll stretches lazily, a habit probably from when she was human.

t_ch: 「走吧，{NICKNAME}，演算结束。」
t_en: "Let's go, {NICKNAME}. The extrapolation has ended."

t_ch: 你尝试重新接入「权杖」系统，但一无所获。
t_en: You attempt to reconnect to the Scepter system, to no avail.

t_ch: 看来你们的运气不太好，奇物竟然被突然出现的微型虫洞吞掉了。
t_en: Looks like you guys are down on your luck. The Curio has been swallowed by a sudden micro-wormhole.

t_ch: 波尔卡挥下手术刀，充斥寰宇的欢笑声，和压榨算力的强制干涉，使她的数据体濒临瓦解。无论是对她还是黑塔，这都将是最后一棋。
t_en: Polka swings the scalpel. The mad laughter resonating across the cosmos, along with the forced interference that squeezed at the computational power, causes her data model to teeter on the verge of collapse. For both her and Herta, this will be their final move.

t_ch: 大人们说，世界就要毁灭，连绵的冰川在重力牵引下撞击大陆，碾过地表。而后，战前关怀员的封冻将使所有生灵陷入沉寂。
t_en: The adults say that the world is going to be destroyed, and that the endless glaciers will crash into the continent, obliterating the surface under the pull of gravity. Then, the frozen seal of the Pre-Battle Caregiver will plunge all life into eternal silence.

t_ch: 但你笃信，这些都只是虚假的、惑乱人心的话语。你感到分外寒冷，打起哆嗦，而这也是假象，是试图固化认知的陷阱。
t_en: But you firmly believe that this is no more than the fake and misleading words of man. You feel an intense cold and begin to shiver, but this is just another illusion, a trap trying to enforce these ideas in your mind.

t_ch: 你闭上双眼，尝试脑海里描摹出真实的世界。
t_en: You close your eyes and attempt to picture the real world in your mind.

t_ch: 没有寒冷。
t_en: There is no cold.

t_ch: 没有死亡。
t_en: There is no death.

t_ch: 没有战争。
t_en: There is no war.

t_ch: 一个温暖的世界，整个宇宙就封装在一个瓶子里，没有外星球、外星人。那些骇人耸听的故事，都是编出来骗小孩的把戏。
t_en: A world of warmth, where the entire universe is sealed inside a bottle. There is no such thing as other planets or aliens. They are all sensational stories made up to trick children.

t_ch: 那些口口声声说见过外星人的外交官呢？他们只是给其他的东西取名叫外星人而已。比如，一台叫外星人的吸尘器，相对认知的诠释。
t_en: All of those diplomats that swear that they have seen aliens before? They are simply naming things they have seen as "aliens." For example: A vacuum cleaner known as an alien. Another example of relative cognition at work.

t_ch: 你听到高楼坍塌的声音，自己正在坠落，但是不怕，你仍能给予它新的解释。
t_en: You hear the sound of a tall building collapsing and find yourself plummeting to the earth below, but you are not scared. You can just give it another interpretation.

t_ch: 我变成了一片叶子。
t_en: I have become a leaf.

t_ch: 我缩小成一只蚂蚁。
t_en: I have shrunk to the size of an ant.

t_ch: 我正陷入一场噩梦。
t_en: I am having a nightmare.

t_ch: 疼痛，只要不睁开眼，就一定是错觉。你继续描摹世界，能让所有人都幸福生活下去的，真实的世界。
t_en: If you close your eyes, all pain is no more than an illusion. You continue to picture your world, the real world in which everyone can live happily ever after.

t_ch: 窗外的哭喊声，不过是鸟儿们的啸叫，它们在迎接一场盛大的婚礼。冰冷的液体滴落到你的腿上，你感到一阵心惊。
t_en: The screams you hear out the window are simply the calls of birds celebrating a lavish wedding ceremony. A freezing liquid drips onto your leg as you feel a shock.

t_ch: 千万不要睁眼！
t_en: Do not open your eyes!

t_ch: 一旦睁眼，就会被虚假的现实欺骗。你蜷缩着身子，向虚无的深处沉没，属于自己的，封闭而美好的雪景球。
t_en: The moment you open your eyes, you will be taken in by the deceit of the false reality. You curl into a ball and find yourself pulled deep into the depths of the void, into your own personal, sealed-off, and perfect snow globe.

t_ch: 又一个自转周期，异乡的微小生命在你的表皮上涂抹亮色，「凯旋而归」，这是独属于他们文化的花纹，从动作来看，代表亢奋、进食和捕猎的信息。
t_en: Another sidereal day. A microscopic life form from another land paints colors on your skin. "Triumphant return," it is a pattern unique to their culture. Their movements seem to communicate information on excitement, feeding, and hunting.

t_ch: 他们并不分享你携子嗣带来的猎物，相反，你乐于见到他们为你引来更多的蠹虫。「乐于」，这是你在演化中得到的，最具智慧的态度。
t_en: They do not share in the prey that you bring in with your offspring. To the contrary, you are pleased to see that they are attracting more insects to your location. This act of "taking pleasure" is the most intellectual attitude function you have developed during evolution.

t_ch: 继续进食。
t_en: Continue feeding.

t_ch: 蠕动身体。
t_en: Wriggle.

t_ch: 在原地发呆。
t_en: Stay completely still.

t_ch: 他们为你的巢穴浇筑外壳，以防天敌的袭击。甲壳上尖锐的犄角耸立，不时迸发出热量与火光，他们就寄居在这些犄角里。
t_en: They construct an outer shell for your nest to protect it from natural predators. Sharp horns protrude from the carapace, occasionally emitting heat and flames. They live inside these horns.

t_ch: 你有时会把多余的唾液分给他们，用于加固犄角。这样，你的子嗣便能获得一个更温暖的巢穴。
t_en: Occasionally, you will offer excess saliva to them to help fortify the horns. This way, your offspring will have a warmer nest to live in.

t_ch: 为了后代的繁衍。
t_en: For the prosperity of the next generation.

t_ch: 为了更好的生存。
t_en: For greater survival.

t_ch: 虽然他们总做出一些你无法理解的事情，但你的脑蛋白天生不适合处理这种问题。他们在你打造的一颗颗天体上都留下了足迹。而这种足迹会带来更多的子嗣——不过，你也无法处理这样的问题。
t_en: Although they are constantly doing things that you cannot understand, your brain proteins were not designed to handle problems such as these. They leave footprints on every celestial body you create, and these footprints will bring greater offspring... But this is also a question that you cannot handle.

t_ch: 你想为孩子缔造一个居所，让种族的血脉不断延续。「共生，共存」，你常常能听到他们呼喊类似的词句。
t_en: You wish to create a home for your children, to allow the blood of your species to continue on. You often hear them calling out slogans like: "Coexistence and co-survival."

t_ch: 我并不抵触。
t_en: I don't disagree.

t_ch: 我喜欢这种生活。
t_en: I like this life.

t_ch: 我没有这样的思考能力。
t_en: I have no mental capacity to question this.

t_ch: 你瘫软的身体浸入巢穴，沿着通路下潜。异种生物修筑的透明外壳让通路变得更为坚固，他们站在那里，欣赏你的沉没。而你只想要好好睡一觉。
t_en: Your limp body sinks into the nest, flowing down through tunnels. The transparent outer shells crafted by alien creatures have fortified the passages as they stand by, admiring your sinking form. All you long for is a good sleep.

t_ch: 睡眠，有机体如此称呼这一过程。连贯性意识的中断，对你而言，也可以将其视为死亡。是的，你是已经死去的巨人，筑城者留下的无数机器中的一台。
t_en: Sleep. That is what organic organisms call it. A break in the continuity of consciousness, it could also be seen as a form of death. That's right, you are a deceased giant, just another of the countless machines left behind by the Architects.

t_ch: 但你正感受着自我的存在，庞大的身躯，密集的热源分布在周身的各处。你曾大得像一座山脉，现在的你比山脉还要广阔。
t_en: But you can currently sense your own existence. Your gigantic body has concentrated heat sources distributed across it. You were once the size of a mountain range, and now you are even vaster.

t_ch: 我是一个世界。
t_en: I am a world.

t_ch: 我是一段美梦。
t_en: I am a dream.

t_ch: 我是一缕亡魂。
t_en: I am a ghost.

t_ch: 你睁开眼，如一片遮蔽海洋的云翳在转瞬间散去，星光投射在你的传感器里。无论将视线往什么地方移动，能看到的，只有一望无际的星海。
t_en: You open your eyes. A great nebula disperses in front of your eyes like clouds over a vast ocean as starlight hits your sensors. No matter where you cast your gaze, all you can see is an endless sea of stars.

t_ch: 嘿！难道你是飘在宇宙里的太空垃圾吗？
t_en: Hey! Could it be that you're space trash floating around in the universe?

t_ch: 你迫切地想要知道，那些曾受你保护的人去了哪里？为了替他们赎回笑容，你甚至献出了最后的机心。
t_en: You desperately want to know where the people you once protected have gone to. You even sacrificed your last mechanical heart to buy back their smiles.

t_ch: 过去多久了？
t_en: How long has passed?

t_ch: 他们都离开了。
t_en: They have all left.

t_ch: 他们都消失了。
t_en: They all disappeared.

t_ch: 「大块头，往里面看！」喧闹声在回路深处响起，你感到些许烦躁，但更多的却是安心。你将目光转移向自己的内部，绚烂的光球上，巨大的城市屹立其间。在那里，渺小的有机体们在向你挥手。
t_en: "You big idiot, look in here!" You hear a call resound deep in your circuits, which irritates you slightly, but brings a greater sense of relief. You cast your gaze inside yourself, and atop a shining sphere of light stands a great city. In this city, tiny organic organisms wave back at you.

t_ch: 你终于理解了现状，整颗星球，所有的笑声，它们都在你的身体里，成为你的心脏和眼睛，构成机器新的使命。
t_en: You finally understand what has happened. The entire planet and all of their laughter are now inside your body. They have become your heart and eyes and formed a new mission for this machine.

t_ch: 我成功了。
t_en: I succeeded.

t_ch: 大家都活下来了。
t_en: They all survived.

t_ch: 在古老的过去，你送了一颗心给他们。所以，他们也送了你一颗星星。
t_en: In the ancient past, you gave them a heart. Now, they have given you a star.

t_ch: 一场突兀的宴会，扎入体内的针管，拘束的枷锁和实验舱壁。倘若早知道，在老人面前展示祂的赐福，会被幕布后的诸位「邀请」至此，你是否会做出相同的选择。
t_en: An impromptu gathering, a needle that shoots into your skin, chain restraints, and the experiment pod walls. If you had known sooner that showing THEIR blessings to the elder would have won you an "invitation" here from the people behind the curtain, would you have made the same choice?

t_ch: 毫无疑问，这样的事已经历了千遍万遍。
t_en: Without question, I have experienced this a million times already.

t_ch: 你望向舱壁外，垂垂老矣的资助者，他甚至没有力气与你四目相对。
t_en: You look outside the pod at the aged man funding the operation. He doesn't even have the strength to look you in the eyes.

t_ch: 你会后悔的。
t_en: You will regret this.

t_ch: 贪婪会招来厄运。
t_en: Greed will bring disaster.

t_ch: 不死是祝福，也是诅咒。
t_en: The blessing of immortality is also a curse.

t_ch: 没人对你的警告做出回应。
t_en: No one responds to your warning.

t_ch: 强烈的刺痛将你层层包裹，仿佛要撕开你的脉搏，将你晒干成入药的粉末。骇人的生命力沿着血浆和药剂流进他们的躯体。首先是实验体，它被划开的身体逐渐愈合，移植的肿瘤被健康的细胞吞噬。
t_en: A powerful, stinging pain envelops your body. It feels as if your very veins were being torn open, and you were being reduced to medicinal powder. The terrifying vitality flows into their bodies with your blood plasma and medicinal elixirs. First is the test subject, whose slashed open body begins to gradually heal, their transplanted tumor devoured by healthy cells.

t_ch: 然后是病人，他惬意地享受着这个瞬间，与「死亡」道一声永别。
t_en: Then is the sick patient, who rejoices at his eternal farewell with "death."

t_ch: 又一场灾难。
t_en: Another disaster.

t_ch: 又一群蠢货。
t_en: You are all fools.

t_ch: 又一个罪孽。
t_en: Another sin of man.

t_ch: 闹剧的罪魁祸首骤然抽搐，节肢动物的附足，软体动物的触手，植物的枝条，浑浊的生命力自他的皮肤上源源不断地涌出。
t_en: Suddenly, the face of the man that instigated this disaster begins to contort as anthropoid limbs, mollusc tentacles, plant vines, and a stale, fetid life force come bursting endlessly from their skin.

t_ch: 扯断的注射器把药剂撒到护工和研究员身上，暴涨的生物质将实验舱的外壁挤碎，他们哀嚎着，肿胀的身体相互交融，刺进对方脆弱的皮囊。
t_en: The snapped syringe leaks the elixir onto the bodies of the nurses and researchers as the resulting explosion of biomass crushes the experiment pod walls. They cry out in suffering, their swollen bodies blending together and piercing each other's fragile skin.

t_ch: 这就是你们想要的药。
t_en: This is the medicine you asked for.

t_ch: 生长啊，生长啊，不死的怪物冲出实验室，蹒跚地爬进立宪国的广场。此刻开始，又一颗星球被宣判死亡。
t_en: Grow, grow! The immortal monster bursts out of the laboratory and staggers into the plaza of the constitutional state. It was at this moment that another planet was pronounced dead.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，模拟宇宙更新了。我们给<color=#dbc291ff>「扩展装置」</color>加了一个新的演算分支。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, the Simulated Universe has been updated. We've added a new algorithm branch to the <color=#dbc291ff>expansion module</color>.

t_ch: ——「模拟宇宙：黄金与机械」。
t_en: — Simulated Universe: Gold and Gears.

t_ch: 黄金与机械？
t_en: Gold and Gears?

t_ch: #27鲁珀特，俱乐部里我最感兴趣的家伙之一。它自号帝皇，掀起了寰宇皆知的机械帝国危机——「第一次帝皇战争」。
t_en: For me, #27 Rubert is one of the most intriguing members in the Society. It proclaimed itself to be an emperor and started a crisis in the Machine Empire known throughout the universe — The First Emperor's War.

t_ch: 这次阮•梅想培育「博识尊」，而螺丝咕姆一直对无机生命的起源很感兴趣，于是我们一拍即合，选取了#27鲁珀特死亡前的时间。
t_en: Ruan Mei plans to cultivate Nous, and Screwllum has always been interested in the origins of inorganic lives. So, we quickly found ourselves on the same page and decided to focus on the time before the death of #27 Rubert.

t_ch: 第一步是将「计算」、「逻辑」等概念系统性地提取出来，加以整理，筛选出和无机生命有关的内容。
t_en: The first step is to extract concepts such as calculation and logic systematically. Then, we need to organize them and filter out content related to inorganic lives.

t_ch: 一会儿别大惊小怪，你马上要见证宇宙机器头的历史了。
t_en: Prepare yourself, for you're about to witness the history of the cosmic Droidhead.

t_ch: 有什么可玩的？
t_en: What's there to play?

t_ch: 你体验了就知道。看不见实物，听我干讲也没用，对不？
t_en: You'll know it when you go through it. There's no point in listening to my description without seeing the real thing, right?

t_ch: 听说公司对模拟宇宙项目有兴趣…
t_en: The IPC seems interested in the Simulated Universe project...

t_ch: 他们当然有兴趣，这可是模拟宇宙，谁不喜欢？我已经在和他们谈落地了。
t_en: Of course, they would be. Who wouldn't? I'm already discussing with them about the implementation of the project.

t_ch: 好好测试，加油咯。
t_en: Be a good tester and do your best.

t_ch: 回来了？感觉如何？
t_en: You're back. What do you think?

t_ch: 挺有趣的。
t_en: It's fun.

t_ch: 理所当然。
t_en: As expected.

t_ch: 变化不大。
t_en: I don't see much difference.

t_ch: 好吧，你可以去找斯蒂芬反馈。
t_en: Well, you can give Stephen your feedback.

t_ch: 你身边这位是……
t_en: Who's this beside you...

t_ch: 给你介绍下，来了位客人——公司战略投资部的。
t_en: This is our guest from the Strategic Investment Department of IPC.

t_ch: 您好，{NICKNAME}。我受P<unbreak>-45</unbreak>「真珠」小姐委任，代表战略投资部前来与黑塔女士洽谈合作。
t_en: Hi, {NICKNAME}. I was appointed by Miss Pearl, P<unbreak>-45</unbreak> to discuss our cooperation with Madam Herta on behalf of the Strategic Investment Department.

t_ch: 听闻模拟宇宙的下个研究方向是「第一次帝皇战争」，真珠小姐很感兴趣，因为那也是「边星贸易战争」的年代。
t_en: We heard that the Simulated Universe would do research on The First Emperor's War next. Miss Pearl is quite interested, as the Emperor's War happened in the same era when the Borderstar Trade War took place.

t_ch: 战略投资部是公司众多组织中相对年轻的部门，未曾经历那一时期。如今，部门精英奔行银河，清算各项因「万界之癌」产生的坏账，需要更多史实资料。
t_en: The Strategic Investment Department is relatively new compared to other departments in IPC, so we have no first-hand experience of that history. Now, the best and brightest of our department are traveling around the galaxy to settle bad debts stemming from the Cancer of All Worlds. We need more historical data for that.

t_ch: 我们有意为模拟宇宙项目提供支持，并希望能购买与公司相关的推演数据。
t_en: We'd like to support the Simulated Universe project and purchase the derivation data related to the IPC.

t_ch: 「真珠」是谁？
t_en: Who is Pearl?

t_ch: 不知道。螺丝咕姆好像提起过，那多半是位智械吧。
t_en: No idea. Screwllum seems to have mentioned it before, so it's likely to be an Intellitron.

t_ch: 模拟宇宙名气这么大？
t_en: The Simulated Universe is that famous?

t_ch: 不然呢？
t_en: Of course.

t_ch: 说实话，帝皇战争太久远了，我不觉得能对现在的投资工作有什么帮助——除非他们是办公室坐无聊了，想多看些公司刚成立时的八卦。
t_en: Frankly, the Emperor's War was so long ago that I don't think it'll be any help to modern investment — Unless they've run out of things to gossip about in the office and just want to read about the IPC back when it was first established.

t_ch: 或者——算了，商业斗争我不关心。
t_en: Or... Forget it. I'm not interested in corporate conflicts.

t_ch: 他们愿意掏钱，我没阻止的理由。这事很有趣，我答应了。加油收集公司秘闻吧。
t_en: Since they're willing to pay for it, I see no reason to stop. This is an interesting proposal, so I'll say yes. Do your best and keep collecting IPC secrets.

t_ch: 如何，{NICKNAME}，模拟宇宙挺有趣的，对吧？
t_en: What do you think, {NICKNAME}? The Simulated Universe is pretty fun, no?

t_ch: 在你探索的时候，我产生了一个想法，然后马上去做了：等着也是等着，我决定和这位小跟班分享模拟宇宙的见闻。
t_en: An idea hit me while you were exploring, so I immediately went for it. I decided to share my insights about the Simulated Universe with my sidekick here while I waited for you.

t_ch: 我们打了个赌，关于「帝皇」鲁珀特：历史上，鲁珀特遭破坏，机械帝国覆灭，据说其死亡是公司刺客所为，也有一称是「寂静领主」将其毁坏。
t_en: We made a bet about Rubert the emperor. Historically, Rubert was destroyed and the Machine Empire collapsed. Some purport that Rubert was killed by an IPC assassin, and some claim that it was smashed up by the Lord of Silence.

t_ch: 哪边才是对的？我相信是波尔卡•卡卡目。
t_en: Which version is the truth? I believe it was Polka Kakamond who did it.

t_ch: 赌注是什么？
t_en: What are the stakes for the bet?

t_ch: 我认为前者的可能性是存在的。公司会与「鲁珀特一世」抗争到底。
t_en: I think the first version has a possibility to be true. The IPC would fight Rubert I to the death.

t_ch: 走着瞧吧。如果我赢了，它就会把战略投资部此行的计划一五一十道出——虽然我也没那么感兴趣。
t_en: We'll see. If I win, the sidekick will come clean with the Strategic Investment Department's purpose behind this trip — even though I'm not really interested.

t_ch: 否则，我就把模拟宇宙的产权转让给公司。
t_en: Otherwise, I'll transfer the ownership of the Simulated Universe to the IPC.

t_ch: 那可是「寂静领主」，我怎么可能输？噢，别把这事告诉阮•梅和螺丝咕姆。
t_en: There's no way I'll lose by choosing the Lord of Silence! By the way, please don't tell Ruan Mei and Screwllum about this bet.

t_ch: 这样就告一段落了。关于公司的片段还真不少——小跟班，这些资料够你们用上好久了吧？
t_en: Well, that's gonna be the end of it for a while. There's quite a lot of information related to the IPC. Hey, sidekick, is this enough to last you a while?

t_ch: 非常感谢您的帮助，黑塔女士。尾款支付会在……
t_en: I appreciate your help, Madam Herta. The final payment will be made...

t_ch: 结给艾丝妲就行。我不管这个。
t_en: Sort it out with Asta. I don't deal with money.

t_ch: 你们的打赌……
t_en: About the bet between you two...

t_ch: 哦，显而易见——当然是我赢了。
t_en: Oh. Obviously, I won.

t_ch: 不过另一种观点也不是空穴来风。你应该看到了，在一小段数据中，东方启行出现在「寂静领主」的对面。这两人产生过接触，具体关系不明。
t_en: However, the other version isn't totally baseless. You must have seen that Dongfang Qixing appeared opposite the Lord of Silence in a small section of the data. The two have come into contact before, but their relationship was unknown.

t_ch: 我怀疑，路易斯•弗莱明退居幕后也和他们有关，这就是一段更复杂的故事了……
t_en: I suspect they had something to do with Louis Fleming's retirement. That's another even more complicated story...

t_ch: 等待揭开的谜团太多了，我跃跃欲试呢。谁还关心打赌？
t_en: There are so many mysteries waiting to be solved. I can't wait... Who cares about the bet anymore?
